title: Analytics Specialist
job_description:
An established member-based nonprofit organization is seeking a Data Analytics Specialist to support its growing data and digital transformation initiatives. This role will play a key part in leveraging data to support strategic decision-making, enhance operational efficiency, and improve member engagement across the organization.

The ideal candidate will bring a blend of analytics, data engineering, and business intelligence expertise, with the ability to build scalable data solutions, develop impactful dashboards, and collaborate with stakeholders across multiple business units.

Key Responsibilities
• Design and maintain data pipelines and automated workflows to support analytics and reporting.
• Build dashboards and reports using tools such as Power BI or Tableau to enable data-driven decision-making.
• Develop clean, well-structured datasets and analytics models for cross-department use.
• Support data governance initiatives, ensuring data quality, privacy, and compliance standards are met.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business needs into data solutions.

Qualifications
• 5+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or data engineering.
• Strong SQL and Python skills.
• Experience with Power BI or Tableau for data visualization.
• Experience with data pipelines, ETL processes, or automation tools.
• Experience supporting system implementations or data migration projects.
• Strong communication skills and ability to work in a collaborative team environment.

However, we encourage candidates who may fall outside of this range to apply as well.

About the role

Our growing team is looking for a Senior Data Analyst!

About the role:
We’re seeking a Data Analyst (CRO – Growth) to accelerate experimentation and reporting in support of growth. The successful candidate will co-lead the design, execution, and analysis of high-impact A/B tests, while building reliable dashboards and data pipelines to democratize access to user insights.

Working closely with the Product and Actuarial teams, they will standardize metrics, strengthen data quality, and turn results into clear, actionable decisions. This role requires strong rigor, a data-driven mindset, and influential leadership to align cross-functional teams on testing decisions.

What you'll do here:
• A/B test execution and analysis: Develop measurement plans, launch A/B tests, monitor and analyze results, and quantify business impact.
• KPI standardization: Define consistent experimentation metrics and deliver clear reporting.
• Data engineering: Contribute to scripts/data pipelines that power testing and reporting; improve reliability and speed of data extraction.
• Dashboards: Design and optimize dashboards to track key KPIs and reduce manual work.
• QA and tagging: Perform rigorous QA during implementations, help standardize tagging, and identify bugs and optimization opportunities.
• Governance, best practices, and documentation: Document experimentation architecture and processes; support team understanding and adoption.

What you bring to the table:
• At least 5–7 years of experience building dashboards across varying levels of complexity and technologies.
• Strong experience in Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) and A/B experimentation analysis.
• Knowledge of digital analytics data (Google and Adobe Analytics, clickstream).
• Advanced knowledge of Power BI and Snowflake or Python or Databricks, plus strong curiosity about dashboard best practices.
• Performance-driven, results-oriented mindset; data-driven decision-making.
• Previous experience in large enterprises.
• Demonstrated ability to lead through influence and drive change.
• For candidates located in Quebec, bilingualism is required considering the necessity to interact on a regular basis with English-speaking colleagues across the country.
• No Canadian work experience required however must be eligible to work in Canada.

title: Senior Data Analytics Business Insights Analyst
job_description:
Senior Data Analytics Business Insights Analyst

Location

Toronto, ON

Onsite Flexibility

Hybrid

Contract Details

Position Type: Contract

Contract Duration: 12 months

Start: As Soon As Possible

Pay Rate: C$60 C$75 / Hour

Job Summary

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst who lives at the intersection of Data Engineering and Business Intelligence. This is a "hands-on" technical role for a system-builder who can bypass manual reporting and create automated, scalable data architecture. As the Senior Data Analyst, you will be the primary architect responsible for extracting raw data from your entire digital stack Shopify, Salesforce, TikTok, and Review Platforms and engineering the integration logic required to power high-performance Power BI dashboards. You must be comfortable writing the queries, managing the APIs, and presenting the final strategic insights to the business.

Key Responsibilities

System Integration & Extraction:
• Act as the technical owner for data retrieval. You must be able to pull and sync data from multiple fragmented sources: E-commerce (Shopify / Shopify Plus with Orders, Inventory, and Customer LTV), CRM/Service (Salesforce Service Cloud & Data Cloud), Social Commerce (TikTok Ads Manager and TikTok Shop), and Customer Voice (scraping or API-integrating Review Platforms such as Yotpo and Bazaarvoice).

Data Modeling & Architecture:
• Design and maintain the star schema or data models within Power BI. Ensure that data from social (TikTok) and web (GA4) aligns perfectly with backend sales (Shopify) and customer support (Salesforce).

Advanced Power BI Development:
• Lead the creation of the "Business Command Center." This includes expert-level DAX, Power Query (M), and ensuring sub-second report responsiveness for executive users.

Technical Insights Delivery:
• Bridge the gap between "Raw Data" and "Business Action." You will lead the "Review & Insights" sessions with the brand team, explaining how technical shifts in the data impact the skincare business's bottom line.

Automation & Scalability:
• Replace manual exports with automated ETL/ELT pipelines. Ensure the data ecosystem is robust enough to handle high-growth scaling and seasonal spikes (for example, promotion periods).

Education Requirements
• Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or Data Engineering (required). This is a technical-first requirement; the role requires an understanding of system logic, schemas, and API structures.

Required Experience
• 6 to 8 years of professional experience in a technical data role (Data Analyst, Analytics Engineer, or BI Lead).
• Demonstrated experience working with the Shopify and Salesforce ecosystems simultaneously.

Required Skills
• Expert-level Power BI (Visualizations, DAX, Power BI Service).
• Advanced SQL (Complex joins, window functions, and optimization).
• Experience in E-commerce (specifically Skincare/Beauty) is a major asset. You should understand industry metrics like Burn Rate, Repeat Purchase Frequency, and Product Sentiment Analysis.

Preferred Skills
• Experience with Python for data manipulation or API calls is highly preferred.

About GTT

GTT is a minority-owned staffing firm and a subsidiary of Chenega Corporation, a Native American-owned company in Alaska. As a Native American-owned, economically disadvantaged corporation, we highly value diverse and inclusive workplaces. Our clients are Fortune 500 banking, insurance, financial services, and technology companies, along with some of the nation's largest life sciences, biotech, utility, and retail companies across the US and Canada. We look forward to helping you land your next great career opportunity!

job_description:

The Senior Commercial Data Analyst supports the organization by delivering high-quality reporting, managing core commercial data assets, and producing decision-ready insights. This role is accountable for delivering monthly reporting deliverables and supporting the development, optimization, and continuous improvement of dashboards and other recurring analytical solutions.
• Deliver recurring commercial reporting across multiple analytical platforms (Power BI), ensuring timeliness, accuracy, and consistency.
• Work cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Innovation, Category Sales Development (CSD), and others to support ongoing analytical needs.

• Advanced proficiency with the Microsoft Office Suite.

• Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to engage effectively at all organizational levels.
• Demonstrated planning, prioritization, and project management capabilities.
• Highly organized, responsive, and proactive, with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.

title: Senior Data & Business Intelligence Analyst
job_description:
Date Posted: 03/30/2026
Req ID: 46518
Faculty/Division: Temerty Faculty of Medicine
Department: Vice-Provost Relations Health Care Inst.
Campus: St. George (Downtown Toronto)
Position Number: 00056432

Description:

About us:

Home to over 40 departments and institutes, the University of Toronto's Temerty Faculty of Medicine lies at the heart of the Toronto Academic Health Science Network and is a global leader in ground-breaking research and education, spanning clinical medicine, basic science and the rehabilitation sciences sectors.

Your opportunity:

The Toronto Academic Health Science Network (TAHSN) is a consortium of the University of Toronto and 14 affiliated academic hospitals that focuses on collaborative initiatives aimed at promoting and advancing a shared academic mission of top quality education, innovative research and delivery of high quality patient care.

As Senior Data & Business Intelligence Analyst on the TAHSN team, you will have an opportunity to develop and implement novel data collection, management, analysis and reporting strategies for the network. Through effective collaboration with senior leaders, educators, researchers, and fellow data/business intelligence experts across partner organizations, your contributions will lead to informed decision-making processes by leading the design and development of a robust, reliable, and successful data platform and identifying trends and valuable insights. This newly created role will allow you to collaborate with data and IT leads with the Temerty Faculty of Medicine and the University of Toronto in order to align strategies, tools, and approaches to data governance and management. Your proven record of working with big data and analytics from proof-of-concept to final implementation will enable you to collaborate with colleagues, hospital partners, and health system stakeholders in understanding network wide priorities and developing key metrics that will help advance TAHSN’s collective strategy.

Your responsibilities will include:
• Working with stakeholders within the University and its partners to develop and implement a data management and reporting strategy for TAHSN
• Facilitating meetings to determine client needs and providing consultative advice on data requirements
• Determining optimal data collection methods and analyzing and writing program scripts to extract data
• Developing information management methods to optimize use of data
• Running data queries and identifying and resolving data discrepancies and anomalies
• Building dashboards from scratch in support of key performance indicators
• Producing complex statistical reports and analyzing complex data to inform decision making

Essential Qualifications:
• Master's Degree or acceptable combination of equivalent experience.
• Minimum five years' experience in data analytics and business intelligence supporting data-driven decision making
• Demonstrated experience working with large and complex data sets, including experience with data collection, data extraction and data analysis and its applicable tools
• Experience administering SQL databases (preferably PostgreSQL or SQL Server)
• Demonstrated experience designing and deploying data visualizations and dashboards of key performance indicators
• Experience managing data integrity and quality
• Experience preparing and interpreting statistical and graphic reports
• Experience conducting needs assessments to identify and evaluate systems requirements and translate project plans
• An understanding of PHIPA and FIPPA and knowledge of information security principles and practices
• Advanced proficiency in MS Office Suite (particularly MS Excel), survey tools (Qualtrics, Survey Monkey), business intelligence tools (Tableau, POWER BI, Data Cookbook) and statistical packages (SPSS)
• A desire to seek out, learn and embrace new and emerging technology
• Strong communication and presentation skills, with ability to tailor style based on a diverse audience including executive and technical audiences
• Strong database management, spreadsheet and presentation design skills
• Strong knowledge of data visualization best practices, including data mapping, colour theory, and information design
• An inquiry-based approach to data analysis to address inequitable norms and patterns
• Strong attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy while working with large datasets
• Ability to work in a team environment and exercise tact, discretion, flexibility and sound judgment

Assets (Nonessential):
• Experience working with or liaising with academic hospitals

To be successful in this role you will be:
• Communicator
• Insightful
• Perceptive
• Procedural
• Team player

This is a term position, ending May 2027.

title: Business Intelligence Specialist
job_description:
The Opportunity

Are you looking for a caring, collaborative, values-driven workplace with inspiring teammates and leaders? Do you have the ambition and desire to be the best and thrive at the most impactful global insurance provider in the world? Look no further than Zurich Canada.

If you have experience developing new BI solutions, and are looking for a new challenge, we would love to hear from you.

Zurich Canada is currently looking for a Business Intelligence Specialist to support the delivery of reports, dashboards, and data analysis for assigned business units.
Reporting to VP, Data, Analytics & AI., you will maintain existing reporting environments, respond to ad hoc data requests, and contribute to the development of new BI solutions. BI Analysts are expected to develop strong domain knowledge in their assigned areas and grow toward greater autonomy in stakeholder engagement over time.

This is a unique opportunity to build your knowledge and experience for the future in a supportive environment where your voice matters. This posting is for a new vacancy.
Zurich Canada uses artificial intelligence–enabled tools to support certain aspects of the recruitment process, including the initial review and screening of applications. Artificial intelligence is not the sole basis for candidate shortlisting or selection. All hiring decisions are reviewed and made by qualified hiring professionals.

Zurich follows a hybrid work model requiring three days per week of in-person presence, which may include time in the office or market-facing engagements.

What you will do

• Develop, maintain, and refresh dashboards and recurring reports for assigned business areas
• Respond to ad hoc analytical and data requests from business stakeholders within agreed service levels
• Extract, clean, and validate data from source systems to support analytical deliverables
• Document report logic, data sources, and data refresh processes
• Identify and escalate data quality issues, coordinating with data engineering or source system owners to resolve discrepancies
• Participate in requirements gathering and stakeholder sessions as skills and relationships develop
• Support continuous improvement by identifying opportunities to automate or streamline manual reporting
• Contribute to shared team documentation, data dictionaries, and reporting standards

Job Qualifications - What you bring to the table

Required:

• 5+ years of experience in a data, analytics, or reporting-focused role
• Post-secondary education in Business, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience)

AND

• Proficiency in Power BI for dashboard development and report creation
• Working knowledge of SQL for data querying and basic transformations

Preferred:

• Strong analytical mindset with a high degree of attention to detail and data accuracy
• Ability to manage competing priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment
• Strong communication skills and a collaborative, team-oriented working style
• Exposure to enterprise data warehouse or database environments
• Basic understanding of data visualization and reporting best practices

At Zurich Canada, we are committed to pay equity. We determine compensation based on objective criteria such as skills, experience, and internal equity. The salary for this position is 80,000 - 100,000. This range reflects the expected pay for the role across Canada and may vary depending on factors like office location, specific responsibilities, and individual qualifications. As required by local law, Zurich provides in good faith a reasonable compensation range, but starting salaries may exceed this range based on a candidate’s skills and experience.

Our Culture

• At Zurich, we are proud of our culture. We are passionate about Diversity, Inclusion, Equity and Belonging (DIEB). We want you to bring your whole self to work, and we want our employees to be reflective of the communities in which we live and work. Our DIEB initiatives are creating an environment where everyone feels welcome.

• We care about our employees’ well-being and offer a comprehensive health/benefits plan with varying levels of coverage to suit your specific needs and a competitive total compensation package.

• We understand how important it is to rest, recharge and do the things you love. At Zurich, all employees receive a minimum of four weeks of vacation per year to do just that.

• We also understand that employees require time off for personal reasons. Maybe you have an appointment during a workday, a cultural or religious holiday you would like to observe, or you need time off to focus on your mental health. Zurich employees receive four personal days per year to be used at their discretion.

While we can be proud of the contribution to society Zurich makes through our core business of insurance, we must also give back to our communities through our talent, time and resources.

• We have won numerous awards for our workplace culture. We are proud to be one of Greater Toronto’s Top Employers and to have received Insurance Business Canada’s 5-Star Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Award.

Make a difference. Be challenged. Be inspired. Be supported. Love what you do. Work for us.

Zurich Canada is part of the Zurich Insurance Group, a multi-line insurer with approximately 55,000 employees worldwide serving customers in global and local markets. Zurich Canada has been a leading insurance provider serving mid-sized and large companies, including multinational corporations, in the Canadian commercial market for 100 years. title: Manager, Data Engineer
job_description:
Requisition ID: 257694

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Role

Contributes to the overall success of the CBO Performance Enablement department by ensuring individual goals, plans, and initiatives are executed and delivered in support of the team’s business strategies and objectives. Responsible for supporting the end to end data development and enablement lifecycle, including sourcing and transforming data, building and maintaining reliable data solutions that enable reporting and performance insights, and supporting the underlying data platforms and infrastructure. Ensures all activities are conducted in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies, and established standards and procedures.

Is this role right for you? In this role you will:
• Champions a customer-focused culture to deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems and knowledge.
• Designs, builds, and maintains scalable data architectures and data pipelines using SQL Server (on premise and private cloud) to support periodic data integration, transformation, validation, and enablement of operational and performance reporting. Ensures solutions align with data management principles, technology strategy, and governance standards.
• Works in an Agile environment, adapting quickly to evolving business and technical requirements, tight timelines, and competing priorities. Supports data driven decision making by enabling timely, well structured, and trusted data for downstream analytics and reporting teams.
• Identifies opportunities to automate manual and repetitive data processes, driving continuous improvement in efficiency, reliability, and maintainability through the effective use of tools, standards, and engineering best practices.
• Applies iterative development practices across all phases of data solution delivery, including data profiling, ingestion, transformation, modeling, cleansing, conforming, enrichment, and aggregation, establishing a strong foundation for downstream reporting and performance insights (limited direct visualization work).
• Ensures the availability, reliability, and integrity of data products through monitoring, controls, and support processes. Builds strong partnerships with enterprise architecture, infrastructure, application, and technology teams to meet performance, availability, and support expectations.
• Supports structured analysis and issue resolution by enabling accessible, well designed data assets and participating in root-cause analysis, triage, and remediation of data quality or pipeline failures.
• Proactively recommends and executes improvements to engineering practices, tooling, and delivery approaches to enhance operational efficiency, solution quality, and long term sustainability of data platforms.
• Understands how the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions. Actively pursues effective and efficient operations of his/her/their respective areas, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational risk, regulatory compliance risk, AML/ATF risk and conduct risk, including but not limited to responsibilities under the Operational Risk Management Framework, Regulatory Compliance Risk Management Framework, AML/ATF Global Handbook and the Guidelines for Business Conduct.

Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We’d love to work with you if you have:
• University degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
• 3+ years of hands-on experience in data engineering, business intelligence, or analytics engineering roles, supporting reporting and performance enablement use cases.
• Strong experience designing, developing, and maintaining data solutions using Microsoft SQL Server in on premise and private cloud environments.
• Proven experience with ETL / data integration development using SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS) and Azure Data Factory (ADF) to support scheduled, reliable, and scalable data pipelines.
• Experience working with Power BI, including developing and supporting semantic models, datasets, and reports in collaboration with analytics and business stakeholders.
• Solid understanding of dimensional and relational data modelling, data quality checks, data validation, and performance optimization.
• Experience developing and supporting SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) and SQL Server Analysis Services (SSAS) solutions is required.
• Comfortable working across the end to end data lifecycle, including data sourcing, transformation, validation, and enablement of downstream reporting and analytics.
• Experience supporting production data processes, including monitoring, issue triage, root-cause analysis, and implementation of controls and guardrails to ensure data reliability.
• Exposure to Python and related data libraries is considered an asset.

What’s in it for you?
• A rewarding career path with diverse opportunities for professional development
• A culture that promotes teamwork and cross-functional collaboration to achieve business goals. Inclusive workplace that values diversity of thought, background, and experience
• A competitive compensation and benefits package
• Opportunity to work with a very enthusiastic team that helps our clients by ensuring the Bank is able to continue to operate to deliver key business services.
• Hybrid working environment allowing for work/life balance

Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

title: Data Platform Engineer Specialist
job_description:
Reference no. R2850669

Position Title: Data Platform Engineer Specialist

Department: Platform Engineering (Mesh)

Location: Toronto, ON

About the job

Ready to push the limits of what’s possible? Join Sanofi in one of our corporate functions and you can play a vital part in the performance of our entire business while helping to make an impact on millions around the world.

We are Sanofi, an innovative global healthcare company that chases the miracles of science to improve people's lives. As an R&D-driven, AI-powered biopharma leader, we work across 100 countries to transform the practice of medicine by turning the impossible into the possible. Our expertise spans vaccines, rare diseases, rare blood disorders, neurology, immunology, oncology, diabetes, and cardiovascular diseases. Guided by our core values - Aim Higher, Act for Patients, Be Bold, and Lead Together - we provide life-changing treatment options and life-saving vaccine protection to millions globally while putting sustainability and social responsibility at the center of our ambitions.

Who You Are:

You bring a wealth of experience as a data or cloud engineer, playing an integral role in designing and developing robust solutions that elevate business operations. Your expertise spans both back-end and front-end technologies, and you have a proven record of building scalable, highly functional systems. With a keen eye for user experience, you prioritize meeting user needs through agile development and close collaboration with stakeholders. You’re a clear communicator who thrives in cross-functional teams, enjoys working with subject matter experts, mentors and supports your colleagues, and consistently delivers professional-grade solutions. Driven by curiosity, you’re always eager to learn, innovate, and improve.

Position summary:

Join Sanofi’s data platform engineering team and play a key role in advancing analytics, AI, and ML initiatives that shape the future of healthcare. As a Data Engineer, you’ll design and deliver scalable solutions, mentor fellow engineers, and collaborate closely with leadership and teams to address both immediate and long-term business needs. Enjoy opportunities to learn from experts across technical and business domains, and drive impactful, data-driven innovation.

About Sanofi

We’re an R&D-driven, AI-powered biopharma company committed to improving people’s lives and delivering compelling growth. Our deep understanding of the immune system – and innovative pipeline – enables us to invent medicines and vaccines that treat and protect millions of people around the world. Together, we chase the miracles of science to improve people’s lives.

Main Responsibilities:
• Collaborate closely with experienced data engineers, data scientists, and stakeholders to deeply understand complex data needs—especially in crucial domains like bioinformatics, omics, and clinical data.
• Design, build, and maintain robust data infrastructure on AWS Cloud in support of our data mesh architecture.
• Automate, optimize, and fine-tune platform provisioning, scaling, and maintenance tasks to boost operational efficiency, performance, scalability, and cost control.
• Lead the development and optimization of data pipelines, leveraging your expertise in data integration, ETL/ELT, advanced tooling and AWS cloud to deliver cutting-edge solutions.
• Implement data monitoring and alerting systems, and partner with DevOps teams to proactively identify and resolve data issues.
• Ensure data security, compliance, and governance at every stage of the data platform, following global standards and best practices.
• Establish and enforce global data engineering standards, ensuring alignment with data architecture, platform, quality, and governance principles.
• Demonstrate deep expertise in implementing data warehouses, data lakes, and distributed processing technologies—such as Spark, Hadoop, and Kafka—in production environments.
• Showcase your advanced proficiency with SQL (preferably Snowflake) and both relational and non-relational databases to optimize complex queries and data manipulation.
• Exhibit mastery in programming languages (like Python, Shell scripting, and Scala/Java) for the development of sophisticated data engineering solutions.
• Work within cross-functional agile teams to architect and deploy hybrid-cloud solutions and automated pipelines, ensuring seamless and high-performance data processing.
• Document data infrastructure design, configuration, and processes thoroughly for reference and training purposes.
• Act as a mentor and leader, offering guidance and support to junior engineers, and fostering a culture of collaboration and growth within the team.
• Engage actively within the data engineering community by sharing insights, best practices, and innovations that contribute to broader industry progress.

About You

Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/Master's in STEM or a relevant field with 4-6 years of experience in data engineering, with a strong preference for experience in the life Sciences/pharmaceutical industry.
• Extensive background in designing, developing, and optimizing data and cloud solutions, including data pipelines, service-oriented architectures.
• Strong knowledge of data integration technologies, ETL/ELT, and modern data engineering technologies, with experience in implementing or supporting Data Mesh architectures.
• Experience with multimodal data systems and architectures, including batch, near real-time, and streaming data.
• Proven experience designing distributed architectures for large-scale data processing with high performance, scalability, and fault tolerance (AWS, Snowflake, Spark, Hadoop, Kafka).
• Advanced knowledge of SQL, relational/non-relational databases, and data query optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Shell scripting, and Scala/Java.
• Expertise in managing cloud-native systems following IaC and DataOps principles (terraform, CI/CD, Orchestration, Actions)
• Extensive experience with agile development processes and concepts.
• Exceptional problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
• Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
• Ability to lead teams effectively and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.
• Curiosity and a commitment to continuous learning and improvement.

Nice to Haves:
• Experience in the life sciences/pharmaceutical industry.
• Familiarity with Data Mesh concepts such as data as a product, domain-driven design, and federated computational governance
• Experience building AI agents, chatbots, conversational AI or Machine learning applications.
• Familiarity with AI/ML concepts and how to present AI/ML driven insights to users.
• Familiarity with visualization tools (PowerBI, Tableau) and project management tools (JIRA, Confluence).

If you are an experienced and accomplished Data or Cloud Engineer looking for an exciting opportunity to shape the future of healthcare through data-driven insights and transformative solutions, we encourage you to apply for this key position. Join us in making a significant impact on the lives of global patients and customers through innovative data engineering solutions. We look forward to hearing from you!

Why Choose Us?
• Bring the miracles of science to life alongside a supportive, future-focused team.
• Discover endless opportunities to grow your talent and drive your career, whether it’s through a promotion or lateral move, at home or internationally.
• Enjoy a thoughtful, well-crafted rewards package that recognizes your contribution and amplifies your impact.
• Take good care of yourself and your family, with a wide range of health and wellbeing benefits including high-quality healthcare, prevention and wellness programs.

AI Usage

"Artificial Intelligence” refers to any systems that use automated processes, including algorithms and machine learning, to analyze data and make predictions, inferences, decisions, or recommendations without direct human involvement. These systems may process personal information to identify patterns, improve services, or support decision-making. The Company may use Artificial Intelligence for purposes including, but not limited to, resume screening and hiring, scheduling interviews or meetings, conducting surveys, matching skills with potential job openings, interview scoring, ensuring compliance with regulations applicable to our industry, and activities related to performance evaluation. Information collected and processed by the Company’s Artificial Intelligence systems may include the personal information detailed above and calendar availability. It excludes the information collected and processed for monitoring purposes. You should contact Human Resources if you have a question or concern regarding your personal information. You can also contact Canada’s Privacy Officer via Sanofi’s data subject request portal,  Data Subject Rights Webform. The Data Subject Rights Webform can also be used to request access or correction of your personal information and file a complaint.

Sanofi is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. Our goal is to attract, develop and retain highly talented employees from diverse backgrounds, allowing us to benefit from a wide variety of experiences and perspectives. We welcome and encourage applications from all qualified applicants. Accommodations for persons with disabilities required during the recruitment process are available upon request.

title: Research Data Engineer GEMINI Data Quality
job_description:
This role will contribute to the Vital real-time health data for Trials, Artificial Intelligence and a Learning Health System (VITAL) platform, which is a federally funded, secure, high-performance computing environment being developed in Ontario, Quebec and Alberta that will allow hospital data to be collected every 24 hours, linked, deidentified, and accessed by researchers through a unified portal. VITAL expands upon the GEMINI platform at Unity Health Toronto, which is a collaborative data and analytics platform that allows 35+ Ontario hospitals to accelerate research and quality improvement, leading to excellent hospital care.

The VITAL team is seeking an experienced Data Engineer to join this innovative network. The role of the Data Engineer will be to extract, transform and load data from source systems (EHRs, administrative databases, registries, etc.) into repositories or pipelines so that the data can be used for analytics (reporting, business intelligence, statistical modeling, machine learning, etc.). The scope of this role is end-to-end, such that it includes all activities from data collection, requirements gathering and database analysis, to pipeline design and programming, to ongoing QA and maintenance of data pipelines and target data systems.

The incumbent will play an integral role liaising with various departments at Unity Health Toronto and across VITAL’s collaborating institutions and hospitals, driving operational excellence for both GEMINI and VITAL. This will be a challenging, rewarding, and fast-paced environment with interaction with clinicians, researchers, data scientists, hospital IT team, and hospital leaders. The ideal candidate will possess exceptional understanding of health data systems and technologies, and excellent skills with inter-institutional stakeholders and initiative.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Requirements gathering and data analysis

Engages with stakeholders to understand their analytic/research/business needs, and work collaboratively with them to develop and validate concrete business requirements for data collection and data pipeline design

Documents business requirements as required

Carries out analysis of source system databases to discover the structure, flows, functions, and interdependencies of data within the system using custom written SQL scripts or an enterprise tool

Documents findings and presents them to the stakeholders

Data pipeline development

Based on requirements and analysis findings, conducts detailed interviews with subject matter experts, data scientists, and hospital IT team to support data collection and inform the design of a logical and scalable pipeline model

Documents the data pipeline model using mock-ups or partial outputs as appropriate, and validates the model with stakeholders

Constructs and automates the data pipeline from the validated model

Leverages HL7/FHIR data standards to ingest standardized clinical data into target data models and pipelines

Designs and implements robust error and exception handling procedures

Documents data pipeline architecture

Monitoring and troubleshooting of data pipelines, target data systems

Routinely monitors system logs/alerts for error and exception detection

Manually runs jobs/restarts pipelines when automation fails

Maintains ongoing quality assurance to correct for data drift

Collaborates with the IT Security team to monitor and protect pipelines against data leakage and ensure compliance with healthcare data privacy policies and regulations

Patches/updates data pipeline software, scripting tools, etc. as needed

Routinely monitors data pipeline health and optimizes code when necessary

Qualifications:

Undergraduate degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Biostatistics, or a related discipline, along with relevant experience managing large scale projects, ideally within healthcare data systems and datasets (e.g., EHR, CIHI, clinical registries)

Extensive knowledge in the design and development of data pipelines required

Mastery of SQL as well as at least one of either R or Python required; working knowledge of relational database systems (Postgres, MySQL, etc.) preferred

Experience designing and running testing scenarios required

Knowledge on Linux commands and Shell scripts required

Working knowledge of version controlling and collaboration platforms (GitHub, GitLab etc.) required

Good knowledge of data warehouse concepts and experience with BI/DW concepts e.g., facts, dimension, star/snowflake schema structures, 3NF modeling, metadata management etc., required

Knowledge of HL7, FHIR standards, Integration Engine, and FHIR server preferred

Familiarity with cloud architecture (AWS, Azure, GCP, etc.) preferred

Experience with data orchestration (e.g. Airflow) and containerization technologies preferred

Good judgment and understanding of what issues to escalate, resolve on your own, making suggestions for possible resolution required

Ability to learn new technology expediently

Ability to maintain responsibility for all aspects of pipeline development, automation and improvements/operations for a project

Ability to work effectively in a team environment and across all organizational levels, where flexibility, collaboration and adaptability are important

title: Senior Business Intelligence Engineer
job_description:
The Reporting, Insights & Analytics team is on a mission to harness the power of data to guide strategic decisions and improve business operations. Reporting directly into our Chief Data Office, this team collaborates closely with C-Suite executives and Business Unit leaders to develop products, refine processes, and generate actionable insights on a global scale.

As a member of this team, you will play a key role in delivering scalable, high-quality business intelligence solutions that power decision-making across Sales & Pipeline, Engagement Economics, Capacity Planning, and other core operational areas. You will contribute to the development of reports, dashboards, and data models that help leadership monitor performance against Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and drive continuous improvement.

This role requires strong analytical thinking, foundational BI engineering skills, and the ability to collaborate effectively with both technical teams and business stakeholders. It is ideal for someone who is passionate about data, eager to grow within a modern cloud data environment, and motivated to contribute to enterprise-wide analytics transformation.

Role Overview

As a Business Intelligence Engineer, you will support the design, development, and optimization of enterprise reporting and analytics solutions within Kroll’s Azure-based data ecosystem.

You will work closely with senior BI engineers, data engineers, and business stakeholders to translate business requirements into intuitive dashboards and well-structured semantic models. This role provides hands-on exposure to Power BI development, data modeling best practices, and structured agile delivery.

You will contribute to building reliable, scalable, and trusted reporting assets that enable leadership to make confident, data-driven decisions.

Key Responsibilities:
• Own the development and ongoing enhancement of Power BI dashboards and reporting products aligned to business priorities, including clear KPI definitions and decision-oriented storytelling.
• Design, build, and maintain Power BI semantic models using dimensional modeling best practices (e.g., star schema) to improve performance, usability, and consistency across reports.
• Develop reusable, well-documented DAX measures (including time intelligence patterns where appropriate) and optimize calculations and model design to ensure responsive, scalable reporting experiences.
• Perform data shaping and transformation using Power Query (M) with an emphasis on maintainability and repeatability of data preparation steps.
• Use SQL to extract, validate, and analyze data from enterprise systems; partner with upstream teams to resolve data quality issues and reconcile metrics.
• Collaborate with Data Engineers to understand upstream pipelines and data products, provide reporting requirements, and influence data structures that best support analytics and semantic modeling.
• Implement and support governed access patterns, including Row-Level Security (RLS) where required, and partner with platform/tenant stakeholders to ensure the right balance of access and control.
• Manage operational reliability of published assets (refresh requirements, dependencies, and impact), using platform capabilities such as endorsement, lineage, and refresh options (including incremental refresh where appropriate).
• Contribute to BI engineering standards: workspace organization, naming conventions, documentation, and lifecycle practices (dev/test/prod promotion, controlled releases, versioning), consistent with enterprise lifecycle management approaches.
• Document KPI logic, data definitions, semantic model design decisions, and report usage considerations to support governance, transparency, and auditability (not just “how,” but “why”).
• Partner directly with business stakeholders to clarify requirements, translate business questions into analytic specifications, and communicate tradeoffs clearly; Kroll job postings emphasize the importance of translating technical details into business terms and proactively surfacing issues before they reach downstream consumers.
• Participate in Agile delivery routines (planning, refinement, and iterative delivery) and proactively manage your workstream to deliver high-quality outcomes on time.
• Mentor or support the development of more junior analysts/report developers through reviews, pairing, and reusable assets, consistent with enterprise enablement practices

Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 3–6+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Analytics Engineering, Reporting, or a related data-focused role, with demonstrated ownership of end-to-end BI deliverables (requirements model/report build validation release iteration).
• Strong hands-on experience with Power BI, including semantic model design, DAX, Power Query, visualization, and management/security
• Strong SQL skills and comfort working with relational data structures; ability to validate metrics and troubleshoot discrepancies.
• Solid understanding of dimensional modeling principles (facts/dimensions, star schema) and how model design impacts performance and usability in Power BI.
• Experience working in or alongside a cloud-based data platform environment (Azure preferred); familiarity with common Azure analytics components is a plus (ex. Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Lake.)
• Practical knowledge of data governance fundamentals (definitions, documentation, lineage/impact awareness) and a commitment to accurate, trusted reporting.
• Experience with CI/CD processes for Business Intelligences is a plus (ex. Deployment pipelines, Git)
• Strong analytical problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to communicate clearly with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Statistics, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).

About Kroll

Join the global leader in risk and financial advisory solutions—Kroll. With a nearly century-long legacy, we blend trusted expertise with cutting-edge technology to navigate and redefine industry complexities. As a part of One Team, One Kroll, you'll contribute to a collaborative and empowering environment, propelling your career to new heights. Ready to build, protect, restore and maximize our clients’ value? Your journey begins with Kroll.

Join the EDC Team! At EDC, we support Canadian businesses to succeed globally. We provide the financial tools and expertise they need to explore new markets, reduce risks, all towards the goal of making Canada and the world better through trade.

Final date to receive applications: April 22, 2026

• Salary range: $96,557 to $128,743 annually, based on qualifications and experience, plus a performance-based incentive.

Location:
• Export Development Canada operates in a hybrid work environment, currently requiring employees to work in the office 2 days per week. (subject to change).
• This role can be performed from EDC’s headquarters in Ottawa.

About EDC

Export Development Canada (EDC) empowers Canadian businesses to succeed globally. As a financial Crown corporation, we offer innovative financial solutions and expert insights to help businesses explore new markets, mitigate risks, and achieve growth.

Why Join EDC?
• Comprehensive Benefits: EDC offers a competitive compensation & benefits package, work-life balance, and the opportunity to help make Canada and the world better through trade.
• Work-Life Balance: EDC offers a competitive compensation package & work-life balance, hybrid work options, 3 to 4 weeks paid vacation, a corporate closure period, summer early Fridays, and no meeting Fridays.

Take advantage of continuous learning opportunities, including training programs, workshops, and language training.
• Inclusive Culture:
Be part of a diverse and inclusive workplace that champions employment equity and values diversity of ideas, strengths, and backgrounds.
• Wellness Programs:
Access to wellness initiatives, mental health support, and fitness programs to keep you healthy and happy.
• Community Engagement:
Participate in volunteer opportunities and give back to the community through social responsibility programs.

Team Overview

The Digital & Technology Solutions (DTS) group, led by the Chief Information Officer, was established in 2023 with the mission of empowering customers and colleagues to take on the world by delivering secure and reliable digital experiences. DTS has set out to achieve the following objectives for EDC:
• Define, execute, and sustain the integrated technology target state and data model to enable EDC’s 2030 business transformation.
• Establish and manage a rolling 3-year Digital Roadmap that sequences technology outcomes across the organization.
• Keep pace with industry trends and emerging technologies to stay relevant in the market and grow Canadian global trade.

Corporate Functions Transformation – ERP Program

The Corporate Functions Transformation (CFT) program is a multi-year initiative to modernize and unify core business processes across HR, Finance, Procurement, and IT, including the implementation of a new ERP platform.

The EDOT (Enterprise Data Operations and Technology) group under DTS impacts EDC’s operations by ensuring the enterprise data ecosystem runs smoothly, delivering reliable data to the business and supporting strategic initiatives. Within EDOT, the Data Delivery Enablement team optimizes the availability of timely, accurate data to enable data-driven decisions.

The Senior Data Analyst acts as a liaison between business and technical teams to develop data solutions that support business needs. Responsibilities include eliciting, analyzing, communicating, validating, and translating data requirements into agile documentation (EPIC, Features, Stories, Tasks) and data transformation rules. This role requires strong data modeling expertise and the ability to design data integration streams or updates to existing pipelines.

The Senior Data Analyst participates actively in an Agile team and collaborates with stakeholders to maximize opportunities to leverage information and technology for business use cases. The role requires digital proficiency, rapid learning, strong IT knowledge, and excellent communication and analytical skills, plus collaboration and relationship management skills.

What You Will Be Doing
• Understanding client requirements and translating them into technical terms and user stories.
• Collaborating with Agile teams, Business Analysts, Data Architects, and Data Engineers to develop solution approaches.
• Reviewing and recommending conceptual, logical, and physical data models for architectural fit.
• Researching and documenting data platforms and outputs, including source-to-target mapping, business rules for data transformation, data cataloging, and data lineage.
• Analyzing system data elements and existing data structures, understanding data lineage from source to target, and ensuring efficient data flow.
• Utilizing end-to-end data process knowledge to cleanse and enrich data elements for accuracy and consistency.

Performance & Governance

Job Description

Reporting to the Director, Finance Data Analytics and Reporting, the Manager, Finance Data Analytics and Reporting leads the provision of innovative and sought-after insights that drive our business forward through partnership and collaboration. The Manager influences and engages the business decision-making with actionable and value-add data-driven insights assumptions and initiatives, from the financial lens of business partnering, acting as a strategic business partner to other lines of business through its support provided to the Finance Business Partners.

The Manager influences and engages the business owners through financial and data analysis and modeling, development of strategic to tactical actionable insights, measurement and reporting of the business’ financial performance and elaboration of look-forward financial planning views through analytics. The Manager acts as a change agent partnering with finance and business process owners to story tell the business and financial results and drive business performance for its area of responsibility that has undergone and will continue to receive evolving complexities and growing demands for business intelligence.

Job Responsibilities

Below are the main job requirements and responsibilities for the Manager, Finance Data Analytics and Reporting.
• Contributes to the value-add and agile decision-making provided by the Finance Business Partners. Implements the creation of business analytics and insights and look-forward financial views, preparation of analysis of key business decisions and the impacts and risks involved. Implements the maintenance and enhancement of finance performance and planning models and tools, generation of Financial Performance metrics and KPIs measurement and reporting, development of financial plans and forecasts, and delivery of management reporting.
• Supports the broad range of cross-functional business partners across the company through analyzing and creating agile and near-real time insights from streamlined and standardized data, reporting and analytics.
• Partners collaboratively with the Finance Business Partner team who leads the exchanges with business owners, build and maintain business relationships, and to be accountable for the business partnering messages and narratives, supporting the business owners’ decision-making.
• Develops and presents insights from the measurement of financial performance and planning performance to the Directors, General Managers, Canada Post Group of Companies, and other key business owners in the management of Canada Post. Ensures they are provided with the necessary information to support their decision-making and to facilitate their understanding of how these decision impact the approved objectives and corporate priorities.

Job Responsibilities (continued)
• Accountable for determining financial costing of custom value propositions, as part of the preparation of analysis of key business decision and their impacts and risks. Maintains and enhances the sales’ custom business case costing determination processes. Supports the sales’ custom-bid process held by the Pricing Leads. Maintains and enhances finance performance and planning models through the lens of profitability management. Generates financial performance metrics and measurement and reporting KPIs. Supports the development of financial plans and forecasts and delivery of management reporting.
• Manages a Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) business area of responsibility. Aligns with the Finance Analytic Hub, leveraging data governance, reporting and analytics’ frameworks, and business processes and procedures. Defines data to model and report architecture for finance transformation. Develops sought after insights by examining data sets, finding trends, and drawing conclusions. Builds analytics capabilities, up the maturity curve to address lag, and develop leading indicators (descriptive, diagnostic, predictive and prescriptive analytics).
• Leads the development of plans, budgets and forecasts for all expenses, assets, and liabilities in relation to their supported business area(s), in alignment with strategic, tactical and operational plans and targets. Creates and continuously optimizes the supporting planning and financial performance models and scenarios, business insights, and financial views.
• Manages a data-driven team within the FP&A community in the finance function, in accordance with our Finance Target Operating model and through best practices from the industry. Facilitates the development of professional FP&A staff, with functional and/or technical analytics skillsets, that is suitably trained and well motivated to provide high quality services to the organization.

• Completed post-secondary education in a related field, commerce, finance, economics, accounting, business administration.

• CPA (CA, CMA, CGA) professional designation is an asset

• Completed Master of Business Administration (MBA) degree is an asset

Experience

• 3 to 5 years of relevant functional experience.

• 2 or more years’ experience in a supervisory role.

• Experience working in a large enterprise, including Canada Post, is an asset

• Experience with data analytics is an asset

Other Candidate Requirements
• Solid Business and Financial acumen with proven ability to understand and decipher the business and financial issues
• Excellent team building and leadership skills and experience.
• Sound organizational, analytical, judgment and decision-making skills with proven abilities in problem solving.
• Solid financial modeling skills, within a Financial Planning & Analysis focus, and ability to convert into actionable value-add insights
• Knowledge of visualization tools such as Power BI and Power query.

• Programming experience (e.g. Python or VB) and database programming using SQL

• Solid interpersonal skills with an ability to build and maintain relationships.
• Strong oral and written communication skills.
• Proficient computer skills and competent with Microsoft Office programs, ERP financial planning solutions and data analytics & visualization tools.

Our Values - Trust, Respect and Deliver represent our fundamental promise to ourselves, our expectations of one and another and our shared duty to our country.

title: Power BI and Data Integration Developer
job_description:
Dentons is designed to be different. Our Firm leads the way in a rapidly changing legal marketplace. We challenge the status quo and deliver consistent results as well as uncompromising quality and value to our clients. Our global presence is renowned as a Firm with over 21,000 individuals in more than 200 offices serving clients across 80+ countries.

Dentons Canada is committed to its people and communities. We are consistently recognized as an employer of choice having received numerous awards including being selected as one of Canada’s Top 100 Employers (2026). Canada’s Top Employers for Young People (2026), and Canada’s Best Diversity Employers (2026).

This role is an opportunity for you to join Canada’s Global Law Firm, a Firm that offers opportunities to build your career while growing your skills and deepening your expertise.

POSITION SUMMARY

Are you passionate about transforming business intelligence and data integration? We are seeking an experienced, hands-on Power BI and Data Integration Developer to lead our organization’s BI initiatives. This role is responsible for the development and administration of Microsoft Power BI solutions, management of data pipelines, and ensuring data quality and governance across integrated sources. You will also play a vital role in supporting and training teams to maximize their use of Power BI and related tools. Candidates should hold or be in pursuit of Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer or Analytics certifications (DP-600/DP-700) and demonstrate proficiency with the Microsoft Graph API for data integration.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
• Power BI Development & Administration: Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and interactive reports to enable data-driven decision-making. Create and optimize data models, visualizations, and DAX calculations for insightful and performant reporting. Administer the Power BI environment, including workspaces, data gateways, and user access, following best practices for governance and security.
• Data Integration & Management: Oversee all datasets and data pipelines required for BI solutions using Microsoft Fabric components (OneLake, Dataflows Gen2, Data Factory pipelines, Lakehouse/Warehouse). Integrate data from multiple sources—databases, cloud services, enterprise APIs, and files—ensuring consistent ETL/ELT processes for analysis. Monitor and optimize data refresh schedules and troubleshoot dataflows for reliability.
• Data Modeling & Quality Assurance: Develop robust semantic data models (star schemas, relationships, calculation groups) for reusability and single-source-of-truth reporting. Implement data validation, error checks, and row-level security (RLS) to ensure data accuracy, privacy, and integrity. Establish and enforce data governance standards and documentation for BI datasets and integrations.
• Stakeholder Collaboration & Training: Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate business needs into BI solutions. Conduct workshops and maintain ongoing communication with non-technical teams. Provide user training and support to maximize Power BI adoption and champion a data-driven culture.
• Performance Optimization: Optimize BI solutions for performance and scalability. Tune data models and SQL queries for faster load times and query responses. Leverage DirectQuery/Import vs. Direct Lake modes appropriately and implement features to enhance report usability and performance.
• Emerging Technologies & Strategy: Stay current with Microsoft BI and data integration advancements. Evaluate and recommend new tools (Azure Synapse, Databricks, AI integrations) to continuously improve BI infrastructure. Utilize the Microsoft Graph API to integrate enterprise data from Microsoft 365 and Azure AD, and contribute to the organization’s overall data strategy and analytics roadmap.

QUALIFICATIONS
• Education & Experience: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field. 3-5+ years of hands-on experience in business intelligence development and data integration, including substantial Power BI experience in a production environment. Experience with Microsoft Fabric (lakehouses, data pipelines, or Fabric-enabled Power BI datasets) required.
• Technical Skills: Advanced Power BI (Desktop and Service), DAX, Power Query, and data modeling. Strong SQL (T-SQL) skills for querying and transforming large datasets. Familiarity with Microsoft Graph API, RESTful web service integrations (JSON, REST APIs), and Azure data platforms (Data Factory, Synapse, SQL Database, Data Lake Storage). Experience with implementing data security (RLS/OLS).
• Certifications: Microsoft Power BI and Fabric certifications (e.g., PL-300, DP-600/DP-700) are highly valued. Microsoft Graph or Power Platform training is a plus.
• Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills: Ability to analyze complex data from multiple sources and design effective analytics solutions. Knowledge of data warehousing principles and troubleshooting experience in BI systems.
• Soft Skills: Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Proven ability to work with cross-functional teams and deliver solutions that meet business needs. Experience in training or coaching team members or end-users on BI tools and data literacy. Proactive, self-driven, and able to manage multiple projects and deadlines.

ADDITIONAL QUALIFICATIONS (NICE-TO-HAVE)
• Programming & Scripting: Experience with Python or other scripting languages for data automation, and familiarity with PowerShell or C# for Microsoft Graph API or Power BI administration.
• Alternate BI/Analytics Tools: Experience with Tableau, Qlik, Databricks, or Snowflake is an asset.
• Azure and Cloud Ecosystem: Deep understanding of the Azure ecosystem (Data Lake, Synapse, Machine Learning) and integration with Power BI and Fabric. Familiarity with Apache Spark or Databricks for data processing and CI/CD tools for BI deployments.
• Domain Knowledge: Experience in legal or professional services data environments is a plus.

ADDITIONAL TOOLS & TECHNOLOGIES
• SQL and Databases: Mastery of SQL and experience with relational databases (SQL Server, Azure SQL Database). Knowledge of data warehousing solutions (Azure Synapse, SQL Server Analysis Services).
• Azure and Cloud Services: Familiarity with Azure Data Factory, Synapse, Databricks, and Data Lake Storage (OneLake).
• Programming/Scripting Languages: Proficiency in Python (for data manipulation and Graph API integrations) and PowerShell (for Power BI automation).
• Power Platform: Experience with Power Apps and Power Automate to complement Power BI dashboards and automate workflows.
• Other BI/Analytics Tools: Awareness of Tableau, Qlik, R, and Excel Power Query/Pivot is beneficial.
• By joining our team, you will lead the way in developing and managing sophisticated Power BI dashboards and data integrations, ensure knowledge transfer through team training and support, and help drive our organization’s adoption of the latest Microsoft data technologies for enhanced reporting and insight generation. If you’re ready to elevate our data-driven culture, we want to hear from you!

We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

This is a new role. The target salary range for this position is $120,000 - $130,000, commensurate with the successful candidate’s skills, experience, and qualifications. Dentons is committed to equitable and competitive compensation. This target range falls within our internal salary ranges, which are based on comparable roles in the external market. We strive to hire, promote, and transfer individuals at the entry point of the range, with consideration given to prior experience, relevant skills, and market conditions.

We recognize that rewarding exceptional talent goes beyond salary. Our comprehensive total rewards package includes inclusive and flexible benefits that support the wellbeing and development of our people. This includes extended health and mental health benefits, paid time off, retirement savings plans, a fitness subsidy, parental leave top-up, and more.

In addition, our learning and development programs—such as mentorship opportunities, employee networks, and leadership development initiatives—are designed to support personal and professional growth.

At Dentons, all candidate screening, assessment, and hiring decisions are made by people. From time to time, we use AI-enabled tools to assist in enhancing our recruitment process and materials; however, these tools do not screen candidates or make hiring decisions.

Note: Availability of benefits and programs may vary depending on your location and employment type and may be subject to eligibility criteria. Dentons reserves the right to modify or discontinue offerings, in whole or in part, at its discretion and without prior notice.

Our Purpose

At Deloitte, our Purpose is to make an impact that matters. We exist to inspire and help our people, organizations, communities, and countries to thrive by building a better future. Our work underpins a prosperous society where people can find meaning and opportunity. It builds consumer and business confidence, empowers organizations to find imaginative ways of deploying capital, enables fair, trusted, and functioning social and economic institutions, and allows our friends, families, and communities to enjoy the quality of life that comes with a sustainable future. And as the largest 100% Canadian-owned and operated professional services firm in our country, we are proud to work alongside our clients to make a positive impact for all Canadians.

By living our Purpose, we will make an impact that matters.

--

What will your typical day look like?

As an Analyst on the AI and Data team, you'll collaborate with cross-functional experts to solve complex client problems at the intersection of business and AI. Your day could include:
• Facilitating workshops or stakeholder interviews to understand strategic challenges.
• Designing future-state visions and building use cases that apply Artificial Intelligence, Generative AI, and Advanced Analytics in creative, value-generating ways.
• Supporting the development of Proofs of Concept, governance models, and roadmaps to turn ideas into action.
• Contributing to storytelling through decks, written briefs, and compelling visuals to help clients align on their AI strategy.
• Working alongside data scientists, engineers, and strategists to define and deliver data-driven solutions across industries like financial services, healthcare, energy, and the public sector.

You’ll thrive if you're energized by ambiguity, complexity, and the opportunity to bridge the gap between technical teams and business leaders.

About The Team

We are Canadia’s premier AI and Data consulting practice, with over 300 professionals across Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver, Ottawa, and Calgary. We're shaping the future of AI by combining cutting-edge research, real-world delivery, and human-centered design.

Our team is multidisciplinary, including strategists, technologists, designers, and data scientists. We tackle some of the most pressing and complex challenges across:
• Enterprise AI strategy and GenAI applications
• AI governance, privacy, and ethics
• Advanced analytics, intelligent automation, and AI-powered operating models
• Industry-specific innovation across public sector, healthcare, finance, and customer experience

We’re collaborative, fast-moving, and future-focused – and we’re looking for people who are too.

Enough About Us, Let’s Talk About You

You are someone who speaks the language of both business and AI. You don’t need to code like a developer, but you should understand what’s possible (and what’s not) with modern AI, data, and analytics. More importantly, you can translate that understanding into meaningful business impact.

Good-to-have:
• An undergraduate and/or master’s degree from an accredited institution
• Strong interest in AI, data, or analytics, demonstrated through projects, coursework, internships, or extracurriculars
• Strong organizational skills with ability to work independently and follow through on complex tasks
• Confident communicator with strong verbal and written skills, and the ability to adapt your style to different audiences
• Proficient in PowerPoint and Excel (or equivalent tools to communicate ideas clearly)
• Comfortable with ambiguity, fast-paced environments, and collaborative problem solving

Nice-to-have:
• Prior internship or co-op experience in consulting, strategy, business analytics, or a relevant sector
• Sector experience in public sector, financial services, life sciences and healthcare, natural resources, or customer/marketing
• Knowledge of data governance, information architecture, data engineering, and data visualization
• Experience or education in data science, advanced analytics, or machine learning, including GenAI, Agentic AI, LLM or similar emerging technologies

Total Rewards

The salary range for this position is $50,000 - $90,000, and individuals may be eligible to participate in our bonus program. Deloitte is fair and competitive when it comes to the salaries of our people. We regularly benchmark across a variety of positions, industries, sectors, targets, and levels. Our approach is grounded on recognizing people's unique strengths and contributions and rewarding the value that they deliver.

Our Total Rewards Package extends well beyond traditional compensation and benefit programs and is designed to recognize employee contributions, encourage personal wellness, and support firm growth. Along with a competitive base salary and variable pay opportunities, we offer a wide array of initiatives that differentiate us as a people-first organization. On top of our regular paid vacation days, some examples include: $4,000 per year for mental health support benefits, a $1,300 flexible benefit spending account, firm-wide closures known as "Deloitte Days", dedicated days of for learning (known as Development and Innovation Days), flexible work arrangements and a hybrid work structure.

Our promise to our people: Deloitte is where potential comes to life.

Be yourself, and more.

We are a group of talented people who want to learn, gain experience, and develop skills. Wherever you are in your career, we want you to advance.

You Shape How We Make Impact.

Diverse perspectives and life experiences make us better. Whoever you are and wherever you’re from, we want you to feel like you belong here. We provide flexible working options to support you and how you can contribute.

Be the leader you want to be

Some guide teams, some change culture, some build essential expertise. We offer opportunities and experiences that support your continuing growth as a leader.

Have as many careers as you want.

We are uniquely able to offer you new challenges and roles – and prepare you for them. We bring together people with unique experiences and talents, and we are the place to develop a lasting network of friends, peers, and mentors.

The next step is yours

At Deloitte, we are all about doing business inclusively – that starts with having diverse colleagues of all abilities. Deloitte encourages applications from all qualified candidates who represent the full diversity of communities across Canada. This includes, but is not limited to, people with disabilities, candidates from Indigenous communities, and candidates from the Black community in support of living our values, creating a culture of Diversity Equity and Inclusion and our commitment to our AccessAbility Action Plan, Reconciliation Action Plan and the BlackNorth Initiative.

Our recruitment advisors and hiring teams will utilize human screening combined with AI technology to help identify the skills and qualities that matter most to our business, while safeguarding your privacy and using AI responsibly.

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. As a Data Analyst you will play a key role in analyzing our operational data to improve our Quality Verification strategies for the EA Sports titles (such as FC, NBA and NHL). You should be a sharp, curious, and analytic individual who has a passion for diving into data to solve business problems. Your insights will influence how we build and verify the quality of our games, and how to improve our business processes through data insights.

The Data Analyst will be responsible for assembling data from multiple sources, conducting analyses, performing research, interacting with different internal customers, designing, documenting, and implementing analytical solutions, as well as compiling and presenting results, in graphical and written summaries, to a variety of audiences. You will be responsible for building metrics reporting systems and for tracking process improvements across the organization. You will excel in a fast paced environment, managing multiple responsibilities and deadlines in an internationally based team. The ideal candidate would be an effective communicator who combines solid technical skills with pride in excellence, who also demonstrates the potential to grow into a client-facing consulting role and/or an analytical consulting role (i.e. development of simulation models).

• Assembles data from multiple sources, conducts analyses, performs research, designs, documents and implements analytical solutions.
• Presents analysis conclusions to the internal customer in a clear, understandable, and valuable way.
• Communicates with team members and business stakeholders.
• Presents high quality reports and findings in Power BI or as presentations.

• Conducts analysis using statistical software, databases, spreadsheets, and reporting tools
• Designs, evaluate and monitor key metrics, understanding root causes of changes in metrics
• Develops and maintains scripts and queries to import, manipulate, clean, transform data from different sources
• Builds and shares live dashboards and reports complete with user documentation
• Builds and trains statistical models for hypothesis testing
• Performs quality assurance on generated results to ensure accuracy and consistency
• Presents conclusions in a clear and concise way

The next great Data Analyst needs:
• Relevant experience in a past analytical role.

• Data Analysis - good knowledge of R language, MS Excel mastery, including VBA, PivotTables, and array functions - or other similar tools used for discovering data patterns and correlations through statistical methods.
• Good math and analytical skills including statistics, simulation, and optimization.
