job_description:
• This role is open to remote based candidates based in Canada with a preference for PST*
We’re looking for a Data Operations Analyst to join our team and be part of our next stage of growth. As our Data Operations Analyst, your top priority is turning data into clear, actionable insights that help our Senior Leadership Team make informed decisions. You will work across Finance, Sales, and customer facing teams to deliver reliable reporting, analyze business performance, and surface trends that support operational strategic planning.

This role combines hands-on data analysis with cross-functional collaboration and a high level of ownership over operational reporting. You will be responsible for extracting, analyzing, and presenting data primarily from Salesforce, while identifying opportunities to improve reporting, data quality, and operational visibility across the organization.

Who we are
At Traction Rec, community is at the heart of everything we do. We empower nonprofit organizations like YMCAs, Jewish Community Centers (JCCs), Boys & Girls Clubs of America (BGCA), and Salvation Army Kroc Centers to strengthen their communities through great technology built on the Salesforce platform. By enabling these organizations to deliver their services more efficiently, we help them focus on creating meaningful connections and driving positive change where it matters most.

We believe that business is a powerful force for good. As part of this belief, we’ve committed to the Pledge 1% for the Planet, dedicating 1% of our time, product, profit, or equity to supporting environmental and community causes. This commitment reflects our dedication to creating a positive impact that extends beyond technology.

Our passion for community and innovation has earned us recognition, including:
• Salesforce Nonprofit Sales Partner of the Year and ISV Partner of the Year
• Globe & Mail's Top Growing Companies of 2024 and 2025
• Deloitte's Technology Fast 50 winner 2024
• Deloitte's Technology Fast 500 winner 2024 and 2025

At Traction Rec, we live our values: Community, Authenticity, Impact, Fun, and Growth. As a Living Wage Certified employer in British Columbia, we are dedicated to supporting not just the communities we serve but also the people who make it all happen—our team.

If you’re passionate about making a difference and want to join a company that puts community first, we’d love to have you on this journey with us. Together, let’s create stronger, healthier, and more connected communities.

Skills
Traction Rec’rs come from a variety of backgrounds, and we are eager to find people that are from diverse backgrounds. For this role, you might have:
• 3+ years of experience working with SQL, relational databases, and large datasets in a SaaS or technology environment
• Strong experience working with Salesforce data, including reporting, data extraction, and analysis using tools such as SOQL, Data Loader, or APIs
• Advanced Excel skills including Power Query, pivot tables, formulas, charts, and data analysis
• Experience building reports, dashboards, and visualizations using BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
• Experience working with SaaS business metrics such as ARR, pipeline, churn, retention, and product adoption
• Experience leveraging AI-assisted tools to support data analysis, research, and reporting workflows
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify patterns, root causes, and opportunities for improvement
• Solid business acumen with the ability to connect operational, financial, and customer data to business outcomes
• Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights for stakeholders
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate across teams in a fast-paced environment
• Bonus points if you’ve worked in the nonprofit sector or with community-based organizations

What You Will Be Doing
• Serve as the primary point of contact for data and reporting requests across teams and the Senior Leadership Team.
• Own Salesforce reporting and data extraction for key KPIs, especially around usage and revenue.
• Pull, join, and analyze data from multiple sources to support reporting and decision-making.
• Build, maintain, and improve recurring and ad hoc reports using Salesforce and other analytics tools.
• Track and analyze key business metrics, including churn, customer lifecycle, and product adoption.
• Support Finance, Sales, Product, and customer-facing teams with data for planning, forecasting, and performance tracking.
• Prepare accurate and timely reporting that supports executive dashboards, quarterly business reviews, and board reporting.
• Identify opportunities to improve data quality, reporting processes, and operational visibility.
• Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders across the organization.

Compensation
At Traction Rec, we are committed to a fair, market-based and equitable compensation structure. Our market data is refreshed on an annual basis to reflect the most current market conditions.

The starting salary band for this role is $80,000-$90,000. Starting salaries will vary within this range based on experience, skill level, and internal equity related to the role.

Please note that the range details above reflect the base pay only and do not include our competitive bonus program and benefits that we offer.

Our Approach to AI
At Traction Rec, we’re big fans of AI and we embrace it as a tool that helps us work smarter and more creatively. It may even have helped with this job description. That said, our recruiting process is 100% human. From resume review to interviews to job offers, every step is led by real people who care about real people. No bots making hiring decisions here.

If you’re interested, so are we! Send your resume our way, and let’s chat.
At Traction Rec, we believe that diversity and inclusion are essential to our success. We are committed to building a team that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve, and we actively seek and welcome candidates from all backgrounds. Our hiring process is designed to be fair, welcoming, and respectful to all candidates, regardless of race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, ability, or any other characteristic protected by law. We believe that diversity brings unique perspectives and experiences that can enhance our team and drive innovation, and we are committed to providing equal opportunity and support to all candidates. We encourage everyone to bring their authentic selves to the interview process and join us in building a diverse and inclusive culture at Traction Rec.

Don’t meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and marginalized groups are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. If you’re excited about this role, but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

Please note that employment is contingent on the successful completion of background and reference checks.

title: Senior Data Analyst - Safety
job_description:
Marketing Statement

Join a company that has been a part of moving Metro Vancouver for over 35 years.

British Columbia Rapid Transit Company Ltd. (BCRTC) maintains and operates the Expo and Millennium SkyTrain Lines from our central facilities in south Burnaby. Our services connect downtown Vancouver to the cities of Burnaby, New Westminster, Coquitlam, Port Moody and Surrey. With the future expansion of both the Expo and Millennium Lines gathering pace, infrastructure renewal and new asset acquisition projects underway, this is an exciting time to join our team of over 1,200 dedicated employees, working on one of the longest fully-automated rapid transit systems in the world.

Our work encompasses roles such as administration, engineering, elevator and escalator maintenance, field operations, vehicle maintenance, and wayside (station and guideway) maintenance.

When you work for SkyTrain, not only will you help move Metro Vancouver, you will also have access to comprehensive benefits including paid vacation, medical benefits, transit passes, training and more. We are a proud equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce.

Come explore what makes us one of BC’s Top Employers!

Responsibilities

RIMARY PURPOSE

Responsible for the maintenance, analysis, and reporting of data generated for all activities within the oversight of the Rail Safety & Security department, including data relating to incidents, investigations, regulatory compliance, and other aspects of railway safety. Works closely with managers and staff across the department in supporting the development of strategies, programs, and campaigns for improving overall railway safety. Assists the Manager and Director in preparing reports suitable for audiences at different organizational levels.

Key Accountabilities

Maintains and analyzes data relating to railway safety, including safety incidents, safety violations, passenger injuries, incident investigations, and compliance to WorkSafeBC, Technical Safety BC (TSBC), and Transport Canada regulations. Maintains and analyzes data relating to all other aspects of Rail Safety & Security activities, including security, emergency management, and environmental management, and compliance to relevant regulations. Uses a data-based approach to interpret findings and uncover trends.

Collaborates with BCRTC’s Business Analytics team to sort through provided data, define, assess and validate the quality of data and provide proper reports and analytics for the Director, Senior Leadership team meetings, board meetings and the departmental reviews and decision makings.

Actively participates with Rail Safety & Security managers by providing quality data analytic reports in supporting data driven decision makings, developing and implementing various strategies and programs for their areas of responsibility. Works closely with those managers to assist in determining appropriate targets and measures, risk analysis activities, and hazard identification.

Under the direction of the Director and Manager, prepares reports and analyses on Rail Safety & Security topics for managers and staff at different levels of the organization. Ensures reports are clear, concise, accurate, and appropriate for the audience.

Provides guidance, training, and support to all BCRTC departments affected by railway safety incidents and investigations to identify and evaluate potential hazards and ensure the implementation of corrective measures resulting from investigations and railway safety data analysis.

Acts as the BCRTC Railway Safety Data Specialist and liaises with external agencies (such as TSBC), providing recommendations to mitigate and resolve issues, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.

Coaches and provides guidance to team members as assigned.

Qualifications

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE:

The requirements for this job are acquired through completion of a university degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, Engineering or other relevant business-related fields plus seven (7) years of progressive experience in advanced data and analytics functions in a multifaceted organization, including at least one (1) years’ experience in leading comprehensive analytical projects. Master’s, Doctorate degrees, and Professional Certifications in related fields may be considered as equivalent to practical experience. Experience in public service program monitoring and evaluation, or experience in workforce analytics, such as occupational health & safety, training, productivity, or other HR analytics would be considered an asset.

Other Requirements

Solid knowledge of the relevant standards, legislation, acts, and regulatory requirements pertaining to the rail industry.

Advanced knowledge of data analysis software and tools.

Advanced analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.

Strong writing and communication skills to prepare data analyses, reports, and presentations.

Solid time management skills, to balance and coordinate effort across multiple, concurrent activities.

Advanced working knowledge of standard office software, including word processing, spreadsheets, presentations, and web-based systems.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

Work Designation

Resident

This position works predominately on-site.

Rate of Pay

The salary for this position ranges from $84,480 - $126,720 per annum. Actual salary offered will be based on education, experience, skills, and qualifications as they relate to the role.

We aim to provide competitive pay that reflects your professional background and expertise. We want to ensure our offer will align with your strengths considering experience, skills, and internal equity to offer a fair and equitable salary. The Total Compensation Package, which includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan as well as other employment offerings will be discussed in detail as your application progresses.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to http://www.translink.ca/careers to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: April 09 2026

Closing Date: Open Until Filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

SkyTrain is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca.

title: Data Analyst Health Surveillance

title: Data Analyst, Information Technology
job_description:
Full-time, permanent
Position summary
Reporting to the project management office manager, information technology (IT), the data analyst is responsible for collecting, processing, analyzing, and interpreting data to support business and technical decision-making. The data analyst will use advanced qualitative and quantitative methods to identify trends, develop reports, and provide actionable insights that improve systems and processes. This includes but is not limited to regular data quality monitoring, preparing data for analysis and generating regular and ad hoc reports.
Duties and responsibilities

Duties include but are not limited to the following:
• support all data requests:
• review, extract and clean data
• manipulate and merge data
• analyze data
• prepare data summaries
• design and maintain dashboards, reports, charts and graphs as required by operations and key business partners
• monitor system performance metrics and identify trends and anomalies
• analyze data related to system usage, application performance, and operational efficiency
• translate business requirements into technical data analysis and reporting solutions
• maintain and manage databases, data warehouses, and data models
• guide developers or database administrators to design new data structures in line with an organization-wide data dictionary that can facilitate cross-system integrations
• perform statistical analysis to identify patterns, correlations and opportunities for improvement
• ensure data accuracy, integrity and consistency across IT systems
• collaborate with cross-functional teams including software developers, system administrators, cybersecurity specialists, leadership teams, and business analysts
• document data processes, methodologies, and reporting standards
• support data governance and compliance initiatives
• performs any other related duties and participates in special projects as required to support the College of Physicians and Surgeons of BC (CPSBC)
• takes measures to protect all data and information sources to comply with CPSBC’s privacy policy and procedures

Skills and qualifications

Required skills and qualifications include:
• successful completion of a bachelor’s degree, preferably with an emphasis in statistics, computer science, science, data analytics, business analytics, or a related field supplemented by a certificate/diploma/degree specializing in related technologies
• three or more years of experience as a data/information and/or reporting analyst managing and controlling data for IT operations and business teams
• excellent quantitative, analytical, problem-solving and investigative skills
• able to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate a significant amount of information with attention to detail and accuracy
• strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office applications including Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables), Word, Access, SharePoint and Outlook
• strong experience with SQL and relational databases
• strong experience with data analytics tools like Power BI or Tableau
• strong experience with relational database design is an asset
• data warehousing and data lake concept experience preferred
• excellent oral, written and interpersonal communication skills
• detail oriented with strong organization skills to handle multiple concurrent tasks and meet project deadlines
• self-motivated with ability to prioritize and independently execute assigned work as well as ability to collaborate and work within a team
• demonstrated knowledge and application of relevant legislation, ethical and legal obligations, and guidelines relating to privacy, confidentiality, and security of information

The compensation range for this position is based on an annual salary of $72,827 to $91,034 per year.

The College of Physicians and Surgeons of British Columbia (CPSBC) has been recognized as one of BC's top employers since 2011 and as a certified Living Wage Employer in BC since 2025. Our goal is to attract, develop and retain highly talented employees. We offer an excellent working environment including a hybrid work-from-home/in-office model, opportunities for personal and professional growth, and a comprehensive benefits package.
CPSBC is committed to our ongoing journey to be a diverse, equitable and inclusive place to work. All interested applicants, regardless of age, family or marital status, physical or mental disability, race, colour, religion, place of origin, and ancestry, Indigenous identity, gender identity or expression, sex, sexual orientation political belief, socio-economic background, criminal conviction or any other characteristic protected by applicable law, are strongly encouraged to apply. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in the recruitment and selection process.
CPSBC values the strength diversity brings to our workplace, so if you’re excited about a career at CPSBC but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the current job postings or you are not ready to apply yet, we encourage you to create an employee profile in the careers portal and our HR team will be able to see your skills and proactively match you with other roles more closely aligned to your experience and skillset.
We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for interview will be contacted.
CPSBC is located on the unceded and traditional territory of the Coast Salish peoples, including the territories of the hən̓q̓əmin̓əm̓ speaking peoples―the xʷməθkʷəy̓əm (Musqueam) and sel̓íl̓witulh (Tsleil-Waututh) Nations, and the Sḵwx̱wú7mesh-ulh Sníchim speaking peoples―the Sḵwx̱wú7mesh (Squamish) Nation.
We acknowledge the rights and title of BC First Nations whose territories span across the province. These territories recognize that laws, governance, and health systems tied to lands and waters have existed here since time immemorial.

title: Business Intelligence - Advanced Analytics Engineer
job_description:
6-month Analytic Engineer contract role with a team focused on enterprise wide reporting solutions.
• 6-month Analytics Engineer contract.
• Great chance to be part of a large-scale business transformation.
• Primarily in office work environment, 4 days a week in office.

What & Why:
As a result of growth our client is looking to add a contract analytics engineer to their team. This role combines data engineering expertise with business intelligence to deliver enterprise-wide reporting solutions. You'll spend half your time building sophisticated dashboards and analytics for the broader enterprise and half supporting the employee help desk team, requiring someone who can work across the full data stack, from understanding and joining backend tables in Snowflake to creating compelling visualizations in Power BI. You should have a data engineering mindset with the ability to collaborate closely with architects and data analytics teams to design end-to-end reporting solutions, maintain complex data structures, automate analytics capabilities, and translate technical metrics (SLAs, CSAT, FCRR, people analytics) into strategic insights that inform leadership decisions across the organization.

Who:
This client is one of the most recognizable Canadian brands, a true Vancouver success story who has expanded globally. They are well known for their corporate culture and has won major awards for their accomplishments. They have invested majorly in technology and have had an incredibly successful past few years, including a major strategic acquisition. They have an all-star leadership team who keeps you accountable and provides support while still affording you tremendous autonomy. They are located close to rapid transit in newly renovated offices in downtown Vancouver and have a primarily on site work arrangement with 4 days a week in office.

You:
You will bring the following education, skills and experience to the role:
• Strong data engineering and backend technical skills including proficiency in SQL, Python, or R for querying relational databases, with ability to understand complex data structures, join tables, and manage large datasets from multiple systems.
• Hands-on experience building enterprise dashboards and visualizations using Power BI (primary), with ability to support existing sophisticated dashboards and create new reporting solutions regardless of platform.
• Experience with cloud data platforms and analytics tools such as Snowflake (maintaining tables and data structures) and ServiceNow Performance Analytics, with focus on backend data work rather than platform administration.
• Proven ability collaborating with technical teams (architects, data analytics teams, vendors) to design end-to-end reporting solutions, with familiarity in technology support metrics (SLAs, CSAT, ASA, FCRR) and organizational people analytics (scheduling, forecasting, turnover, shrinkage).

Compensation:
This role pays between $55-65 per hour.

Next Steps:
If the sound of this opportunity excites you, and you’re confident that it’s a good fit for your experience and career goals, then we’d love to hear from you! Please send your updated resume to us by applying to this posting and one of our awesome team of recruiters will be in touch.

title: Data Architecture & Business Intelligence Lead
job_description:
Job ID

2447282_pl355

Date posted

04/03/2026

Data Architecture & Business Intelligence Lead

Decision Support Services

Vancouver, BC

The Data Architecture & Business Intelligence Lead reports to the Manager, Data Analytics, Reporting and Evaluation (DARE) and provides direction for the strategic and tactical operations of a multi-disciplinary team responsible for the planning, design, development, deployment, and management of new and existing data warehouse, business intelligence (BI), and analytics solutions, serving designated portfolios of PHSA programs and services. Providing technical leadership and senior-level expertise, the Lead is a professional expert in hospital and clinical workflows, systems data, data warehousing and architecture, promoting analytics solutions and the use of Enterprise Data Warehouse across PHSA to enable the organization's strategy and vision within a complex and dynamic health care environment. The Lead is responsible for both the day-to-day workflow operations and leading assigned projects for the portfolio; mentors, provides guidance and direction, and evaluates staff performance; and assists the Manager and. Senior Leadership with the hiring, performance management, and termination of staff. In collaboration with the Manager, the Lead is responsible for the development, assessment and analysis of an annual operating budget.

What you'll do

Key Accountabilities
• Lead architecture and implementation of analytics solutions to develop, enhance and improve analytics and use of data at various PHSA programs and external stakeholders. This includes leading the high-level conceptual design of complex data marts, multidimensional cubes or other semantic models, reports, dashboards and other front-end interfaces, and providing technical expertise in the development of the solutions by assessing client needs and requirements, and reviewing and approving technical options. Collaborate with peers and team members on conceptual design, development, and implementation of the Enterprise Data Warehouse.
• Supervise Data Warehouse and Business Intelligence Specialists, Business Analysts, and contractors to develop and implement high-quality solutions that meet the Enterprise Data Architecture standards. Establish clear definitions of responsibility for each employee, review workload assignments, and adjust schedules according to workload levels to meet operational and project delivery requirements. Monitor compliance of the teams to an advanced and "best practice" set of technical and data standards across PHSA to improve business intelligence, advance analytics solutions and further research excellence.

VALUES STATEMENT:

Our commitment to the principles of fundamental justice embedded in the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, and to Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion is ambitious and foundational. It informs everything we do, as we defend liberty, equality, autonomy, and dignity; as we dismantle colonization, racism, sexism, ableism, transphobia and homophobia, and all discrimination. These are our values, and they inform our hiring practices too. Our business is aiding people in severe legal distress and necessitates internal and external alignment of practicing these values. Integrating Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion throughout every aspect of Legal Aid BC is critical to building that culture, and we prioritize ongoing resources to achieve that goal.

The Opportunity

As Data Analyst reporting to the Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, this position is pivotal in transforming data into actionable insights through interactive and visual representations, your primary function includes defining and validating data elements and structures, developing subject area models, and maintaining data architecture across multiple systems. In addition, this role is responsible for producing and maintaining various value-add Business Intelligence reports.

Key accountabilities include, but aren't limited to:
• Define and validate data elements and data structures;
• Develop subject area models.
• Maintain data architecture artifacts for multiple systems
• Define and maintain data lineage charts for the organization.

• Design, develop, test and maintain BI solutions, including dashboards and reports, using software life
• cycle.
• Work closely with various team members on multiple concurrent projects.
• Provide input into and maintenance of the centralized reporting strategy for LABC;
• Maintain and adjust existing reports as business requirements change;

Who We Are

Legal Aid BC (LABC) is a non-profit organization established by the Legal Services Society Act in 1979. Our mission is to provide legal information, advice, and representation services to BC's most vulnerable and marginalized citizens who lack the necessary resources to effectively access the justice system when their families, freedom, or safety are at risk. Our vision is to see BC’s legal system transformed, by the way LABC represents our clients, into one more equitable and inclusive with a mission to strengthen client-centred legal help for British Columbians experiencing barriers accessing the legal system.

At LABC, we know that our continued ability to help the people who need us is due to the hardworking individuals who make up our team, dedicated to making a difference in the lives of people in need. We foster a positive culture of open communication for our team to thrive within, underpinned by our values of client focus, advancing Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and change through leadership.

Legal Aid BC is in the early stages of, and committed to a transformative journey of embedding Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion in all aspects of our work. We seek talent from equity-seeking groups, including but not limited to individuals who self-identify as First Nations, Inuit, or Metis descent, persons with diverse abilities, gender diverse, racialized, and members of the 2SLGBTQIA+ community, be a part of this exciting stage of Legal Aid BC’s growth. We care deeply about the culture we are building and are looking for staff members who not only excel in their respective areas but are also passionate about supporting the people we serve and each other. If this resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. All interested and eligible applicants will be considered with due consideration to all protected grounds under the BC Human Rights Code. If we can do anything to make your application experience a positive and equitable one, we want to hear from you on what we can do better.

To understand why legal aid matters, and our commitments to Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, please explore our website, planning and guiding documents at https://legalaid.bc.ca/.

The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is an arm’s-length federal agency responsible for the shared stewardship of the lands and waters that make up the Port of Vancouver, Canada’s largest port. The Senior Business Intelligence (BI) Developer is instrumental in developing enterprise analytics and data solutions and providing technical support for the enterprise data management platform. • Participate in the analysis, and documentation of analytics solutions from design through to implementation.

• Develop data transformation and integration logic to deliver curated datasets and analytical models that support enterprise reporting and decision-making.
• Implement automated data validation and quality checks within data transformation, integration and analytical models to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency of datasets.
• Define and maintain business and technical metadata, including data definitions and lineage, to support data discovery, understanding and reuse.
• Create and maintain enterprise semantic layers to support reporting, dashboards, and analytics.
• Participate in forming strategies for VFPA data availability, security, archival, recovery and migration.
• Design, build and deploy dashboards and analytical reports.
• Develop analytical calculations and business logic to support reporting and self-service analytics.

What you bring:

• A minimum of 5 years of senior BI development experience in an enterprise environment.

• Must have demonstrated experience with database optimization, data replication, database recovery and performance tuning.

• Must have strong SQL skills and experience with analytical expressions (e.g. DAX)

title: Data Engineering, Team Lead
job_description:
Our Story & Purpose:

We’re Vancity, a member-owned credit union built on the principles of inclusion and social justice. Since 1946, our relentless commitment to these values has helped us challenge the status quo and break down barriers. We’ve made bold commitments to become net-zero by 2040 across all mortgages and loans, and we’re actively pursuing strategies in Indigenous banking and financial resilience for our members.

As the largest private sector Living Wage Employer in Canada, we’re proud to be consistently recognized as one of the country’s Top Employers. If you’re ready to join our team of 2,300 diverse individuals, access competitive rewards and benefits, and be part of a greater movement - apply today!

Your Role in Supporting Our Members:

As a Data Engineering Team Lead, you will provide technical leadership and direction for the organization’s data engineering capabilities. The role sets engineering standards, designs scalable and secure data architectures, and leads to complex, cross-team data initiatives. While remaining hands-on, you will influence platform strategy, mentor engineers, and ensure alignment with enterprise architecture, governance, and business priorities.

This is a Full-time, Permanent role based at our Head Office in Vancouver, British Columbia and will report directly to the Director, Data Platforms. Our head office is based in Vancouver, but we are open to candidates in BC and Ontario. While this position is for an existing vacancy and provides a hybrid work arrangement, you will be expected to be on-site for events and business demands when needed.

How You'll Make an Impact:
• Solving highly complex, ambiguous engineering and architectural problems
• Making key technical decisions that influence platform scalability, reliability, and future direction
• Evaluating and selecting tools, frameworks, and patterns with long-term impact in mind
• Balancing technical excellence with delivery timelines and business priorities
• Communicating architectural designs, trade-offs, and recommendations to senior technical and business stakeholders
• Acting as a trusted technical advisor to product, analytics, and platform leadership
• Influencing engineering practices and standards across multiple teams
• Facilitating alignment between business needs and technical solutions
• Operating as a senior individual contributor with no formal people management responsibilities
• Providing mentorship and technical leadership to junior engineers
• Leading by example in code quality, operational excellence, and engineering discipline

What You’ll Bring to the Team:
• Bachelor’s degree in computer science or related technical discipline
• 6-8+ years of industry experience in Data Engineering or related field handling data processing and transformation of disparate datasets
• Experience with big data and analytics programming languages such as Python, R, Spark in Databricks
• Experience with batch and real-time processing frameworks (EventHub, Apache Spark, Apache Kafka, etc.)
• Strong ability to innovate and adapt to emerging technologies
• Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to manage competing priorities
• Experience with Azure Data Lake, Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Semarchy and EventHub
• Exposure to DevSecOps practices and security-first principles
• Understanding of data storage, mapping, modeling, and real-time data handling

You’ll Thrive Here If:
• Strategic and risk-aware – You connect capital, liquidity, and balance sheet decisions to enterprise risk appetite and anticipate emerging vulnerabilities.
• Analytical – You apply rigor to financial modeling and stress testing, challenge assumptions thoughtfully, and deliver clear, defensible insights.
• Collaborative – You build trusted cross-functional relationships and provide independent and objective insights that strengthen decision-making.

We value lived experience, so if you are interested in this role, we encourage you to apply even if you feel your skills don't perfectly align with those listed.

What You’ll Earn:

This role offers a salary range of $113,100-$139,650 per annum. The base pay offered may vary depending on factors such as relevant qualifications, skills, previous experience, and internal equity. As part of our total rewards package, employees may also be eligible for our annual incentive program, subject to program eligibility requirements.
• **Position open until filled***

Why You’ll Love Working Here:

A career at Vancity is more than just a job, you’re joining a tradition of change-makers who are creating lasting change for our communities. Beyond base pay, we offer a comprehensive total rewards package to ensure our employees are empowered to thrive:
• Living Wage Employer: We’re the largest private-sector Living Wage Employer in Canada and consistently ranked among Canada’s Top Employers.
• Customizable Benefits: Permanent employees receive flexible benefit packages that can be tailored annually to meet evolving needs.
• Generous Vacation: New employees start with 3-4 weeks of vacation per year, with additional days earned over time.
• Extra Stat Holidays: In addition to BC’s 11 statutory holidays, we offer 2 extra days, plus care days for personal or family illness.
• Immediate Health Coverage: Health and dental benefits begin on your hire date, with three levels of coverage to choose from.
• Defined Benefit Pension: Our retirement plan provides a guaranteed income for life, recognizing that retirement looks different for everyone.

Vancity Talent Programs:

Vancity supports an inclusive hiring process for candidates who self-identify as Indigenous, Black, or Trans. With special permission from the BC Human Rights Commissioner, this initiative provides access to career development opportunities, prioritized job screening, and feedback. Any information you choose to share will be stored securely and used only for recruitment and career development connected to this initiative, in line with the BC Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA). For details, please see our dedicated Talent Programs job posting.

At Vancity we’re committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive workplace to help our people and communities thrive and prosper. Diversity in our workforce is integral so that we can truly represent, understand and respond to our community needs and deliver on our member experience.

This role is an open vacancy, and our hiring process is grounded in fairness, transparency, and inclusion. We are committed to an inclusive, barrier-free and accessible recruitment experience for all candidates. If you require any accommodations or support at any stage of the recruitment process (including the application stage), we encourage you to let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition team at recruitment@vancity.com. We’re here to work with you to ensure your needs are met promptly and effectively. All requests will be handled with the utmost respect and confidentiality, so you can participate fully in the process.

title: Cloud Platform Engineer
job_description:
Job Description

Location: Vancouver, BC (Hybrid)

Type: Contract – 12 months (with potential extension)

We are seeking a Senior Cloud Platform Developer to help design and evolve a modern cloud-native platform with a focus on developer experience, reliability, and automation.

This role operates as a senior individual contributor responsible for building and supporting platform capabilities across Kubernetes, GitOps, and Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) environments. The successful candidate will contribute to core systems such as CI/CD pipelines, automation frameworks, and platform control-plane services that enable development teams to deliver software efficiently and securely.

This is a hands-on technical role suited for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, takes ownership of outcomes, and prioritizes long-term platform quality, scalability, and

operability.

Responsibilities

Contribute as a senior individual contributor on the platform engineering team.
• Design, develop, and maintain cloud-native platform capabilities.
• Implement observability, governance, and automation frameworks to ensure system performance, reliability, security, and compliance.
• Develop and maintain Kubernetes automation, GitOps workflows, and Infrastructure-as-Code frameworks.
• Ensure systems are resilient, scalable, and cost-efficient.
• Build and support CI/CD pipelines to enable reliable and efficient software delivery.
• Participate in architecture discussions, design reviews, and technical documentation.
• Support incident response, operational readiness, and platform reliability improvements.
• Collaborate with engineering teams to improve developer workflows and platform usability.
• Promote best practices in automation, reliability, and platform engineering.

Qualifications
• 5+ years of experience in platform engineering, cloud infrastructure, or site reliability engineering (SRE).
• Strong programming experience in Python, Go, or C#.
• Strong understanding of Kubernetes architecture and controller frameworks.
• Experience with GitOps tools such as ArgoCD.
• Experience with Helm and Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform or Crossplane).
• Experience implementing automation frameworks for cloud-native environments.
• Familiarity with data infrastructure platforms (ETL pipelines, MLOps, streaming or batch processing).
• Strong understanding of system performance optimization, cost management, and troubleshooting.
• Ability to work independently and drive projects to completion.
• Strong communication and collaboration skills.

Nice to Have
• Contributions to CNCF open-source projects (Kubernetes, Crossplane, ArgoCD, Prometheus, etc.).
• Deep expertise in Kubernetes APIs, CRDs, and control plane development.
• Experience running platforms on Azure, AWS, or GCP.
• Experience with Kubernetes scheduling tools such as Karpenter.
• Experience implementing observability and distributed tracing using tools such as OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, and Grafana.
• Strong documentation and technical writing skills.
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or equivalent experience.

title: Senior/Staff Data Platform Engineer - (Bangkok based, relocation provided)
job_description:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Position Overview

The Data Platform Engineer is responsible for designing, building, and operating a cloud-native data platform built on Snowflake and public cloud infrastructure. This role owns the full lifecycle of the data platform — from cloud environment provisioning and data lake architecture to transformation pipelines, security enforcement, and data sharing — enabling reliable, governed, and scalable data access across the organization.

Key Responsibilities
• Provision and manage cloud environments including storage, networking, and IAM across public cloud platforms (AWS / GCP / Azure).
• Architect and administer Snowflake as the organization's unified Data Lake and Data Warehouse platform.
• Define and enforce Data Access Policies with encryption, IAM, and Snowflake network policies.
• Build and maintain dbt transformation models with Git-based version control across staging and production data layers.
• Define platform standards, and best practices for data ingestion, transformation, and consumption layers.

Required Skills & Experience
• Hands-on experience with at least one major public cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or Azure), covering storage, networking, and IAM.
• Strong Snowflake administration skills including RBAC design, Data Sharing, external tables, and Snowpark.
• Proficiency in dbt (Core or Cloud) for model development, testing, and documentation.
• Experience with Infrastructure-as-Code tooling such as Terraform for cloud resource provisioning.
• Experience with pipeline orchestration using Snowflake Scheduling, Apache Airflow, or equivalent tools.

Scope of Ownership
• Responsible for the end-to-end design, provisioning, and operation of the cloud data platform — spanning raw ingestion zones through to curated, analytics-ready data layers — ensuring reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency across all environments.
• Maintains overall responsibility for the unified data warehouse and lake platform architecture, including performance tuning, resource management, cost governance, and the evolution of the platform as organizational data needs grow.
• Owns the transformation layer from design through to production, ensuring data models, testing frameworks, and documentation are accurate, consistent, and fit for consumption across all downstream use cases.
• Drives the definition and adoption of platform-wide standards covering data ingestion, transformation, and consumption patterns, and evaluates tooling and architectural decisions as the platform matures.

title: Principal AI Research Engineer
job_description:
Huawei Canada has an immediate permanent opening for a full-time permanent Principal AI Research Engineer.

About the Team:

Established in 2012, the Central Media Technology Institute (CMTI) is Huawei's center for media technology innovation and engineering, enhancing the technical competitiveness of media products. Sitting within CMTI, the Graphics Technology Lab focuses on research and breakthroughs in photography, audio, video, computer graphics, XR, and codec standards to maintain the company's leadership in media technology. Our R&D team comprises skilled CG algorithm experts, researchers, software architects, and standards specialists dedicated to building core capabilities for an improved graphics experience.

About the Job:
• Develop and lead projects, team ideas, and mentor junior engineers.
• Conduct advanced research and engineering on AI-based rendering techniques including but not limited to Neural Rendering acceleration, Neural material representation, Neural Texture Compression, etc.
• Transfer advanced research AI/CG solutions to solving real-world problems that will be integrated in various Huawei products.
• Provide expert opinions on relevant fields by summarizing research findings in high-quality papers or patents.

The base salary for this position ranges from $121,000 to $230,000 depending on education, experience and demonstrated expertise.

Job requirements

What you’ll bring to the team:
• Solid industry engineering level and experience over 5 years.
• Industry work experience in CG related field over 3 years with hands-on development.
• MSc / Ph.D. in computer science or related fields with a focus on applied ML to computer graphics or computer vision.
• Extensive Background in machine learning and deep learning, e.g., DNN, GAN, ViT, and Diffusion techniques.
• Experienced in neural representation/rendering such as NerF, Gaussian Splatting, neural rendering, procedural generation, SVBRDF, etc.
• Experienced in Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc. and a track record of translating ideas into research prototypes quickly.
• Published Papers at relevant conferences or Journals such as SIGGRAPH, Eurographics, TOG, CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, TPAMI.
• Experienced programming skills on shading languages, e.g., glsl, hlsl, etc.
• Experienced in building large-scale machine learning solutions for general computer vision/graphics topics including model design, data collection/augmentation evaluation, etc.

title: Power BI and Data Integration Developer
job_description:
Dentons is designed to be different. Our Firm leads the way in a rapidly changing legal marketplace. We challenge the status quo and deliver consistent results as well as uncompromising quality and value to our clients. Our global presence is renowned as a Firm with over 21,000 individuals in more than 200 offices serving clients across 80+ countries.

Dentons Canada is committed to its people and communities. We are consistently recognized as an employer of choice having received numerous awards including being selected as one of Canada’s Top 100 Employers (2026). Canada’s Top Employers for Young People (2026), and Canada’s Best Diversity Employers (2026).

This role is an opportunity for you to join Canada’s Global Law Firm, a Firm that offers opportunities to build your career while growing your skills and deepening your expertise.

POSITION SUMMARY

Are you passionate about transforming business intelligence and data integration? We are seeking an experienced, hands-on Power BI and Data Integration Developer to lead our organization’s BI initiatives. This role is responsible for the development and administration of Microsoft Power BI solutions, management of data pipelines, and ensuring data quality and governance across integrated sources. You will also play a vital role in supporting and training teams to maximize their use of Power BI and related tools. Candidates should hold or be in pursuit of Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer or Analytics certifications (DP-600/DP-700) and demonstrate proficiency with the Microsoft Graph API for data integration.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
• Power BI Development & Administration: Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and interactive reports to enable data-driven decision-making. Create and optimize data models, visualizations, and DAX calculations for insightful and performant reporting. Administer the Power BI environment, including workspaces, data gateways, and user access, following best practices for governance and security.
• Data Integration & Management: Oversee all datasets and data pipelines required for BI solutions using Microsoft Fabric components (OneLake, Dataflows Gen2, Data Factory pipelines, Lakehouse/Warehouse). Integrate data from multiple sources—databases, cloud services, enterprise APIs, and files—ensuring consistent ETL/ELT processes for analysis. Monitor and optimize data refresh schedules and troubleshoot dataflows for reliability.
• Data Modeling & Quality Assurance: Develop robust semantic data models (star schemas, relationships, calculation groups) for reusability and single-source-of-truth reporting. Implement data validation, error checks, and row-level security (RLS) to ensure data accuracy, privacy, and integrity. Establish and enforce data governance standards and documentation for BI datasets and integrations.
• Stakeholder Collaboration & Training: Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate business needs into BI solutions. Conduct workshops and maintain ongoing communication with non-technical teams. Provide user training and support to maximize Power BI adoption and champion a data-driven culture.
• Performance Optimization: Optimize BI solutions for performance and scalability. Tune data models and SQL queries for faster load times and query responses. Leverage DirectQuery/Import vs. Direct Lake modes appropriately and implement features to enhance report usability and performance.
• Emerging Technologies & Strategy: Stay current with Microsoft BI and data integration advancements. Evaluate and recommend new tools (Azure Synapse, Databricks, AI integrations) to continuously improve BI infrastructure. Utilize the Microsoft Graph API to integrate enterprise data from Microsoft 365 and Azure AD, and contribute to the organization’s overall data strategy and analytics roadmap.

QUALIFICATIONS
• Education & Experience: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field. 3-5+ years of hands-on experience in business intelligence development and data integration, including substantial Power BI experience in a production environment. Experience with Microsoft Fabric (lakehouses, data pipelines, or Fabric-enabled Power BI datasets) required.
• Technical Skills: Advanced Power BI (Desktop and Service), DAX, Power Query, and data modeling. Strong SQL (T-SQL) skills for querying and transforming large datasets. Familiarity with Microsoft Graph API, RESTful web service integrations (JSON, REST APIs), and Azure data platforms (Data Factory, Synapse, SQL Database, Data Lake Storage). Experience with implementing data security (RLS/OLS).
• Certifications: Microsoft Power BI and Fabric certifications (e.g., PL-300, DP-600/DP-700) are highly valued. Microsoft Graph or Power Platform training is a plus.
• Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills: Ability to analyze complex data from multiple sources and design effective analytics solutions. Knowledge of data warehousing principles and troubleshooting experience in BI systems.
• Soft Skills: Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Proven ability to work with cross-functional teams and deliver solutions that meet business needs. Experience in training or coaching team members or end-users on BI tools and data literacy. Proactive, self-driven, and able to manage multiple projects and deadlines.

ADDITIONAL QUALIFICATIONS (NICE-TO-HAVE)
• Programming & Scripting: Experience with Python or other scripting languages for data automation, and familiarity with PowerShell or C# for Microsoft Graph API or Power BI administration.
• Alternate BI/Analytics Tools: Experience with Tableau, Qlik, Databricks, or Snowflake is an asset.
• Azure and Cloud Ecosystem: Deep understanding of the Azure ecosystem (Data Lake, Synapse, Machine Learning) and integration with Power BI and Fabric. Familiarity with Apache Spark or Databricks for data processing and CI/CD tools for BI deployments.
• Domain Knowledge: Experience in legal or professional services data environments is a plus.

ADDITIONAL TOOLS & TECHNOLOGIES
• SQL and Databases: Mastery of SQL and experience with relational databases (SQL Server, Azure SQL Database). Knowledge of data warehousing solutions (Azure Synapse, SQL Server Analysis Services).
• Azure and Cloud Services: Familiarity with Azure Data Factory, Synapse, Databricks, and Data Lake Storage (OneLake).
• Programming/Scripting Languages: Proficiency in Python (for data manipulation and Graph API integrations) and PowerShell (for Power BI automation).
• Power Platform: Experience with Power Apps and Power Automate to complement Power BI dashboards and automate workflows.
• Other BI/Analytics Tools: Awareness of Tableau, Qlik, R, and Excel Power Query/Pivot is beneficial.
• By joining our team, you will lead the way in developing and managing sophisticated Power BI dashboards and data integrations, ensure knowledge transfer through team training and support, and help drive our organization’s adoption of the latest Microsoft data technologies for enhanced reporting and insight generation. If you’re ready to elevate our data-driven culture, we want to hear from you!

We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

This is a new role. The target salary range for this position is $120,000 - $130,000, commensurate with the successful candidate’s skills, experience, and qualifications. Dentons is committed to equitable and competitive compensation. This target range falls within our internal salary ranges, which are based on comparable roles in the external market. We strive to hire, promote, and transfer individuals at the entry point of the range, with consideration given to prior experience, relevant skills, and market conditions.

We recognize that rewarding exceptional talent goes beyond salary. Our comprehensive total rewards package includes inclusive and flexible benefits that support the wellbeing and development of our people. This includes extended health and mental health benefits, paid time off, retirement savings plans, a fitness subsidy, parental leave top-up, and more.

In addition, our learning and development programs—such as mentorship opportunities, employee networks, and leadership development initiatives—are designed to support personal and professional growth.

At Dentons, all candidate screening, assessment, and hiring decisions are made by people. From time to time, we use AI-enabled tools to assist in enhancing our recruitment process and materials; however, these tools do not screen candidates or make hiring decisions.

Note: Availability of benefits and programs may vary depending on your location and employment type and may be subject to eligibility criteria. Dentons reserves the right to modify or discontinue offerings, in whole or in part, at its discretion and without prior notice.

title: BI Developer Analyst
job_description:
The salary range for this position is CAD $38.88/Hr. - CAD $55.89/Hr.

Summary

We’re hiring a BI Developer Analyst to join
Providence Health Care
and help turn healthcare data into actionable insights. In this role, you’ll build ETL processes, reports, and dashboards that support clinical and operational teams. You’ll play a key part in managing data, ensuring it’s accurate, secure, and accessible, while supporting our HIM Data Management Program.

At PHC, you’ll be part of a mission driven organization known for compassionate care, innovation, and excellence across a network of hospitals and care facilities in British Columbia. You’ll also collaborate with Information Management and IT teams across Lower Mainland Health Organizations to troubleshoot issues and improve systems.

If you enjoy working with data and making a real impact, we’d love to hear from you!

What You Bring

Education
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science (or related field) plus 3+ years of experience in data modeling, ETL, Microsoft BI tools (SSAS, SSRS, SSIS, Power BI), advanced T-SQL, and business requirements analysis, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
• Recent experience coordinating and documenting change management processes for complex information systems.
• Strong business analysis, project management, and relationship-building skills, with excellent interpersonal communication.

Skills and Abilities
• Strong experience with Microsoft BI stack (SSIS, SSAS, SSRS) and Power BI for dashboard design.
• 3+ years of T-SQL development and database querying.
• 3+ years of programming experience (e.g., C#, VB.NET, Java) plus scripting (Python or similar).
• Hands-on experience in ETL environments and data integration across multiple platforms.
• Solid understanding of data architecture, data quality, data mapping, and data structures.
• Knowledge of OLAP technologies and dimensional modeling.
• Ability to translate business needs into technical solutions, including scoping and troubleshooting.
• Experience with HIM business processes and healthcare data systems is an asset.
• Strong documentation, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills, including simplifying complex concepts.
• Proven project management, problem-solving, and teamwork skills, with the ability to work independently and mentor others.

What We Offer

A competitive salary:
The salary range for this position is $38.88 - $55.89 per hour, depending on experience.

Growth opportunities:
We welcome individuals at all stages of their career, offering opportunities for professional development and growth.

State-of-the-art facilities:
The new St. Paul’s Hospital and health campus will be the most innovative approach to the delivery of integrated care in B.C. and Canada, designed to appropriately address the future health needs of patients, families, and our communities. From hospital care to primary and community health solutions, the new St. Paul’s Hospital and health campus will continue to lead innovations in care, research, and teaching. The new St. Paul’s Hospital is expected to open in 2027.

Inclusive culture:
We respect the diversity, dignity and interdependence of all persons. We value the cultural richness that our diverse workforce brings to the care of our equally diverse population of patients and residents.

Your Day to Day:
• Participate in the full development lifecycle of the HIM Data Management Platform (planning, development, testing, deployment, and support).
• Contribute to data model design in collaboration with BI Architect Lead.
• Gather requirements and develop ETL processes, reports, and data integration solutions.
• Validate end-to-end business logic, data mappings, and data quality.
• Deliver ad-hoc and scheduled reports, dashboards, and management tools.
• Maintain and enhance ETL workflows, adapting to changing data sources and business needs.
• Support data migration and ongoing data management within the platform.
• Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve technical and production issues.
• Collaborate with IMITS and business users to resolve system issues and ensure data integrity.
• Provide user support, training, documentation, and technical guidance to team members.

Who We Are:

Providence Health Care is one of the largest faith-based health care organizations in Canada. For the people at Providence, living our Mission, Vision and Values means providing British Columbians with compassionate, socially just, exceptional and innovative care every day.

From our humble roots 129 years ago, Providence has grown into globally renowned research, teaching, and care organization. As individuals within a mission-driven organization, we choose to be part of Providence Health Care because we value the organization’s long commitment to social justice and compassionate care, and its commitment to the process of Truth and Reconciliation with Indigenous Peoples.

We acknowledge that Providence Health Care and the new St. Paul’s Hospital site is located on the traditional, ancestral, and unceded territory of the Coast Salish Peoples, including the territories of the xʷməθkwəy̓əm (Musqueam), Skwxwú7mesh (Squamish), and Səl̓ílwətaʔ/Selilwitulh (Tsleil-Waututh) Nations
.

title: Développeur Principal Business Intelligence (BI)
job_description:
Rejoignez-nous pour permettre le commerce via le plus grand port du Canada

L'Autorité portuaire Fraser de Vancouver est une agence fédérale indépendante responsable de la gestion partagée des terres et des eaux qui composent le port de Vancouver, le plus grand port du Canada. Notre mandat, tel que défini dans la Loi sur la marine du Canada, est de permettre les objectifs commerciaux du Canada au nom de tous les Canadiens, en assurant la circulation sécuritaire des marchandises à travers le port de Vancouver tout en protégeant l'environnement et en tenant compte des communautés locales.

En tant qu'organisation ayant un impact significatif sur les communautés et les entreprises à travers le Canada, l'Autorité portuaire Fraser de Vancouver s'engage à la diversité, à l'inclusion et à la durabilité. Nous nous efforçons de créer un lieu de travail qui reflète la richesse de notre communauté, en donnant à tous les employés les moyens d'atteindre leur plein potentiel.

Le développeur principal Business Intelligence (BI) joue un rôle clé dans le développement de solutions d'analyse et de données d'entreprise et dans la fourniture d'un support technique pour la plateforme de gestion des données d'entreprise. Grâce à une vaste expérience dans la mise en œuvre d'analyses évolutives sur des plateformes de données modernes, ce poste soutient les capacités de données d'entreprise telles que l'intégration des données, l'activation analytique, la modélisation des données d'entreprise, la gouvernance et la qualité des données à travers des architectures hybrides (cloud et sur site).

Voici la position :
• Développe, améliore et soutient des solutions de données complexes, des outils et des automatisations, en partenariat étroit avec des unités d'affaires internes et des équipes techniques
• Consiste à travailler sur plusieurs initiatives d'entreprise axées sur la mise en œuvre de systèmes, solutions et processus nouveaux ou améliorés.
• Traduit les exigences métier en spécifications techniques et met en œuvre des solutions de données et d'analyses sécurisées, évolutives et conformes

Ce que vous faites :
• Travailler avec les parties prenantes de l'entreprise et en interne à travers le département pour définir les initiatives, y compris les livrables fonctionnels et techniques, les estimations de coûts, les besoins en ressources et les bénéfices attendus.
• Participer à l'analyse, à la planification et au développement de feuilles de route détaillées pour la modernisation de la plateforme de données d'entreprise VFPA.
• Partagez la propriété technique de la plateforme de données de l'organisation avec les membres de l'équipe, en soutenant à la fois les environnements sur site et basés sur le cloud.
• Travailler en étroite collaboration au sein de l'équipe des services d'information et au sein des équipes projet pour concevoir, mettre en œuvre et tester des modèles de données et des systèmes de gestion des données.
• Contribuer à la définition et à l'évolution des modèles de données d'entreprise, des normes et des pratiques de métadonnées afin de soutenir une utilisation cohérente et gouvernée des données à travers l'organisation.
• Mettre en œuvre et maintenir des contrôles techniques de qualité des données et de validation au sein de la plateforme de données moderne, des modèles analytiques et des couches sémantiques, basés sur des règles métier définies et des normes de gouvernance
• Soutenir l'ensemble du cycle de vie analytique, y compris la conception des données, l'intégration, la validation et les rapports en utilisant des méthodologies Agile ou Waterfall.
• Veillez à ce que les mises à niveau et modifications de l'infrastructure de données de la VFPA soient alignées sur la norme de niveau de service du département des SI.
• Recherchez de nouvelles technologies, mettez en place des preuves de concept/MVP et faites des recommandations pour construire une infrastructure évolutive et supportable, selon les besoins.
• Effectuer une analyse des causes profondes pour les interruptions de service, développer des solutions à des problèmes techniques complexes et prendre les mesures urgentes appropriées.
• Soutenir la maintenance de l'infrastructure de données : serveurs de base de données, surveillance des performances, configuration de sécurité, logiciels de base de données (SQL) : installation, patch, mises à niveau, démantèlement, etc.
• Assistez aux procédures de sauvegarde des données, de récupération, de haute disponibilité et de reprise après sinistre afin d'assurer la continuité de l'activité.
• Soutenir la mise en œuvre et le maintien des contrôles de sécurité des bases de données (accès utilisateur, rôles et privilèges) ainsi que les fonctions d'audit.
• Prévoyez le stockage et la capacité pour soutenir l'ingestion des sources de données brutes dans la plateforme de données VFPA.
• Surveiller, ajuster et optimiser les performances des bases de données et des rapports.
• Créer ou modifier des objets, procédures et scripts de base de données selon les besoins pour soutenir les exigences analytiques et de la plateforme de données.
• Fournir un support secondaire pour l'administration de la plateforme de bases de données en collaboration avec le DBA principal afin d'assurer la continuité opérationnelle.
• Configurer, effectuer et surveiller les sauvegardes et restaurations au niveau système via des solutions de sauvegarde au niveau entreprise
• Participer à l'analyse et à la documentation des solutions analytiques, de la conception à la mise en œuvre.
• Créer, améliorer et documenter des modèles de bases de données conceptuels, logiques et physiques pour soutenir l'analytique, les rapports et la cohérence des données d'entreprise.
• Développer une logique de transformation et d'intégration des données pour fournir des ensembles de données sélectionnés et des modèles analytiques qui soutiennent le reporting et la prise de décision en entreprise.
• Mettre en place des validations automatisées des données et des contrôles de qualité dans les modèles de transformation, d'intégration et d'analyse des données afin d'assurer la précision, l'exhaustivité et la cohérence des ensembles de données.
• Définir et maintenir les métadonnées commerciales et techniques, y compris les définitions et la lignée des données, afin de soutenir la découverte, la compréhension et la réutilisation des données.
• Créer et maintenir des couches sémantiques d'entreprise pour soutenir les rapports, tableaux de bord et analyses.
• Participer à l'élaboration de stratégies pour la disponibilité, la sécurité, l'archivage, la récupération et la migration des données VFPA.
• Concevez, construisez et déployez des tableaux de bord et des rapports analytiques.
• Développer des calculs analytiques et une logique métier pour soutenir les rapports et l'analyse en libre-service.

Ce que vous apportez :
• Doit posséder un diplôme en technologies de l'information ou un diplôme acceptable dans un domaine connexe
• Un minimum de 5 ans d'expérience senior en développement BI dans un environnement d'entreprise.
• Un minimum de 3 ans d'expérience pratique dans la conception, la construction et le support de solutions de données d'entreprise avec l'environnement Microsoft Fabric, incluant Lakehouse, Warehouse OneLake, l'orchestration Data Factory, la modélisation sémantique et le déploiement dans des environnements de développement et de production.
• Il faut avoir démontré une expérience en conception physique et logique de l'architecture de bases de données pour bases de données relationnelles, entrepôts de données et lacs de données.
• Avoir démontré une expérience en optimisation de bases de données, réplication de données, récupération de bases de données et réglage des performances.
• Il faut avoir une expertise en entrepôt de données et intégration de données : modélisation dimensionnelle, faits, dimensions, tables d'assistance, concepts SCD, etc.
• Il faut avoir de l'expérience avec des modèles de données d'entreprise, des dictionnaires de données, des plateformes de métadonnées/catalogage et des contrôles techniques de qualité des données dans un environnement d'analyse réglementé.
• Doit avoir une expérience démontrée dans le développement de modèles sémantiques d'entreprise et de jeux de données analytiques.
• Il faut bien comprendre les modèles de déploiement de tableaux de bord dans les environnements locaux et cloud.
• Il faut avoir de solides compétences en SQL et de l'expérience avec les expressions analytiques (par exemple DAX)
• Il faut avoir de l'expérience avec Azure DevOps ou le contrôle de version basé sur Git, y compris l'implémentation du pipeline CI/CD et l'automatisation du déploiement.
• À acquérir une expérience avec :

o Langages de programmation fonctionnels (R, Python, etc.), API et services web.

o Principes de sécurité des données, efficacité, qualité et gouvernance.

o Compétences complexes en documentation technique et architecturale.

Ce que nous proposons :
• Salaires de base compétitifs sur le marché et plan d'incitation variable
• Offre d'avantages généreux et inclusifs, y compris un régime de retraite
• Programme de télétravail flexible
• Programme de congé mérité
• Congés payés et soins personnels
• Opportunités d'apprentissage, de développement et d'avancement
• Salle de sport sur place avec service de serviettes dans un site national au cœur du front de mer de Vancouver
• Un lieu de travail convivial, unique et respectueux

Lieu : Vancouver, Canada

Type de poste : Unité de négociation, Temps plein, Permanent, Hybride

Environnement de travail : Conditions normales de bureau et il peut être nécessaire de répondre occasionnellement à des appels hors heures pour des problèmes critiques pour le système.

HABILITATION DE SÉCURITÉ DU TRANSPORT MARITIME

L'occupant doit obtenir et maintenir une habilitation valide de sécurité en transport maritime, car ce poste est requis pour accéder aux zones ou informations restreintes telles que définies par le règlement sur la sécurité des transports maritimes.

Il s'agit d'un emploi basé au Canada, et tous les employés doivent conserver l'éligibilité légale à travailler au Canada. Les candidats progressant dans le processus d'embauche peuvent être soumis à des missions techniques, des évaluations psychométriques et/ou des vérifications des antécédents, y compris, mais sans s'y limiter, des vérifications de casier judiciaire, de crédit et/ou de références.

À la Vancouver Fraser Port Authority, nous sommes dévoués à l'équité en matière d'emploi et à un lieu de travail reflétant la diversité de notre communauté portuaire. Nous accueillons les candidatures de tous les candidats qualifiés, y compris les femmes, les personnes en situation de handicap, les peuples autochtones et les membres des minorités visibles. Un aménagement raisonnable peut être accordé lors du processus de sélection et lors de la collaboration avec l'autorité portuaire. Si vous avez besoin d'aide pour postuler à nos offres ou si vous cherchez des aménagements pour postuler sous un format alternatif, veuillez nous contacter à careers@portvancouver.com. Nous nous engageons à soutenir vos besoins et ferons de notre mieux pour vous fournir l'aide nécessaire.

Nous reconnaissons et reconnaissons que nous sommes situés sur les territoires traditionnels des nations xwməθkwəy̓əm (Musqueam), Sḵwx̱wú7mesh (Squamish) et səlilwətaɬ (Tsleil-Waututh). Pour en savoir plus sur nos relations avec les Premières Nations et notre engagement en faveur de la réconciliation, veuillez consulter notre site web.

title: Data Analyst-Intern
job_description:
Lance ta carrière en data avec une expérience concrète dès maintenant!

Launch your career in data with hands-on experience right now!

À propos du poste

Nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) stagiaire en analytique de données / science des données hautement motivé(e) pour rejoindre notre équipe TI. Ce stage offre une occasion unique d’acquérir une expérience pratique en utilisant les données pour orienter les décisions d’affaires et contribuer au lancement de notre projet Power BI.

Vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration avec des équipes multidisciplinaires afin d’analyser les données, générer des insights et soutenir les stratégies d’affaires dans un environnement dynamique et en constante évolution.

Responsabilités principales
• Nettoyer, analyser et interpréter de grands ensembles de données à l’aide de méthodes statistiques
• Développer et mettre en œuvre des modèles et des algorithmes afin d’extraire des informations pertinentes
• Collaborer avec des équipes multidisciplinaires pour comprendre les besoins d’affaires et les exigences en matière de données
• Créer des tableaux de bord et des visualisations à l’aide d’outils tels que Power BI ou Tableau
• Présenter les résultats à l’aide de rapports et de présentations destinés aux parties prenantes
• Contribuer aux initiatives de qualité des données et à l’amélioration continue des processus

Qualifications
• Connaissance de base des concepts statistiques et des techniques d’analyse de données
• Familiarité avec des outils de visualisation de données (ex : Power BI, Tableau) est un atout
• Excellentes compétences analytiques avec un souci du détail et de la précision
• Capacité à organiser, analyser et interpréter de grands volumes de données
• Excellentes compétences en communication, avec la capacité d’expliquer des concepts techniques à un public non technique
• Personne proactive, autonome et désireuse d’apprendre dans un environnement rapide

Ce que nous offrons
• Programme complet de formation et de mentorat
• Exposition à des technologies de pointe en systèmes embarqués
• Occasion de travailler sur des projets variés avec des clients clés
• Parcours d’évolution de carrière clair au sein de l’organisation
• Environnement de travail collaboratif et innovant
• Programme de bien-être
• Gym sur place et studio de conditionnement physique avec cours en groupe (yoga, aérobie, etc.)
• Espace détente pour vous reposer et vous ressourcer
• Cafétéria subventionnée offrant des repas et collations
• Service de navette du centre-ville de Montréal jusqu’à notre site de Pointe-Claire

title: Business Intelligence Lead

The Business Intelligence Lead plays a pivotal role in shaping the organization’s commercial strategy by harnessing the power of data to generate insights that drive marketing effectiveness, sales enablement, and customer engagement. This role is responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data related to market trends, customer behavior, campaign performance, and competitive activity. By combining business intelligence methodologies with a deep understanding of marketing KPIs and customer segmentation, the manager delivers actionable recommendations that support go-to-market strategies, identify growth opportunities, and enable cross-functional decision-making. • Integrate internal and external data from multiple sources, including Power BI, CRM, Teams channels, and market research reports, into a cohesive reporting structure.
• Develop and maintain dashboards, reporting frameworks, and data visualizations to effectively communicate key insights to stakeholders.
• Maintain consistency and accuracy in reporting across teams, ensuring a shared understanding of key performance indicators.

Analysis and Customer Insights:
• Identify trends, patterns, and correlations within data to uncover opportunities for growth and improvement.

• Assist in the development of predictive models and forecasting techniques to support strategic planning and decision-making, enhancing the organization's ability to anticipate future trends and opportunities.
• Provide historical trend analysis and forecasting inputs to support annual demand planning, promotional strategies, and portfolio investment decisions.

Campaign and Commercial Performance Insights:
• Analyze marketing campaign performance across digital, print, and event channels to identify performance drivers and opportunities for optimization.
• Develop and maintain dashboards and performance reports that provide visibility into product portfolio trends, customer behavior, and marketing ROI.
• Support cross-functional teams by delivering insights on legacy product performance and channel effectiveness.

• Master’s degree or MBA, with a focus on analytics or business intelligence, data science.

title: Tableau Developer
