Tableau Developer

Montreal QA/Halifax Nova Scotia Hybrid

Contract Role

JD for the open positions is as below :

Position Overview

Client seeks an experienced Tableau Developer to join their data analytics and business intelligence team. This role is central to transforming complex financial data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making across the organization. Associate will design, develop, and maintain sophisticated data visualizations and dashboards that empower business stakeholders to understand trends, identify opportunities, and optimize performance in a fast-paced financial services environment.

Key Responsibilities

As a Tableau Developer, you will own the full lifecycle of business intelligence solutions. Your day-to-day activities include designing and building interactive dashboards and reports that translate raw data into compelling visual narratives. You will collaborate closely with business analysts, finance teams, and stakeholders to understand their requirements and deliver solutions that address their analytical needs. You will optimize existing dashboards for performance and user experience, troubleshoot data issues, and provide training and support to end users. Additionally, you will write and refine SQL queries to extract, transform, and load data from multiple sources, ensuring data integrity and accuracy. You will also document your work thoroughly and maintain best practices for version control and development standards.

Required Qualifications

Candidates must have demonstrated expertise in Tableau, with a minimum of three years of professional experience building production-grade dashboards and visualizations. You must possess advanced SQL skills, including the ability to write complex queries, optimize performance, and work with multiple database platforms such as SQL Server, Oracle, or cloud platforms. A solid understanding of financial concepts is essential—you should be comfortable working with financial metrics, basic accounting principles, risk management frameworks, and regulatory reporting requirements commonly encountered in banking. Practical experience with Power BI is a bonus. Strong communication skills are critical, as you will translate technical concepts for non-technical audiences and collaborate with teams across the organization.

Preferred Qualifications

Familiarity with data modeling, ETL processes, and tools such as DataStage or Airflow would strengthen your candidacy. Knowledge of Python for data manipulation and statistical analysis is a plus. Background in banking, capital markets, or financial services, or relevant certifications such as Tableau Desktop Specialist or Microsoft Certified Data Analyst, would demonstrate advanced commitment to the field.

Technical Competencies

You will be proficient in Tableau Desktop and Tableau Server, capable of creating calculated fields, table calculations, and parameters to meet complex analytical requirements. You must write efficient SQL across relational databases and understand query optimization techniques. Your Power BI skills should include experience with Power Query, DAX, and report publishing. You should have working knowledge of data warehousing concepts, dimensional modeling, and star schema design. Familiarity with version control systems such as Git and development workflows is expected. You will also be comfortable with Excel at an advanced level, including pivot tables, VLOOKUP, and data analysis functions.

Minimum 5 years of experience

At Deloitte, our Purpose is to make an impact that matters. We exist to inspire and help our people, organizations, communities, and countries to thrive by building a better future. It builds consumer and business confidence, empowers organizations to find imaginative ways of deploying capital, enables fair, trusted, and functioning social and economic institutions, and allows our friends, families, and communities to enjoy the quality of life that comes with a sustainable future. And as the largest 100% Canadian-owned and operated professional services firm in our country, we are proud to work alongside our clients to make a positive impact for all Canadians.

What will your typical day look like?

As an Analyst on the AI and Data team, you'll collaborate with cross-functional experts to solve complex client problems at the intersection of business and AI. Your day could include:
• Facilitating workshops or stakeholder interviews to understand strategic challenges.
• Designing future-state visions and building use cases that apply Artificial Intelligence, Generative AI, and Advanced Analytics in creative, value-generating ways.

We are Canadia’s premier AI and Data consulting practice, with over 300 professionals across Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver, Ottawa, and Calgary. We're shaping the future of AI by combining cutting-edge research, real-world delivery, and human-centered design.

Our team is multidisciplinary, including strategists, technologists, designers, and data scientists. We tackle some of the most pressing and complex challenges across:

You are someone who speaks the language of both business and AI. You don’t need to code like a developer, but you should understand what’s possible (and what’s not) with modern AI, data, and analytics. More importantly, you can translate that understanding into meaningful business impact.

title: Data Analyst Intern
job_description:
Join Our Team as a Data Analyst Intern!

ABOUT CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES

Carbon Upcycling Technologies (CUT) is a Calgary-based CleanTech company on a mission to transform the cement and supplementary cementitious materials (SCM) industry. We have developed a breakthrough process that upcycles CO₂ and industrial waste streams into high-performance SCMs — reducing the embodied carbon of concrete while improving material performance.

As one of the most innovative companies in the global decarbonization space, CUT is advancing from laboratory science to commercial deployment. You will join a passionate, multidisciplinary team working at the intersection of materials science, process engineering, and climate impact.

WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT

This isn't your typical desk internship. As a Data Analyst Intern at CUT, you will:
• Build hands-on experience in data structuring, cleaning, and dashboard development, working with complex technical datasets from materials and equipment systems
• Contribute directly to improving data quality and visibility, enabling more informed decisions in plant operations and performance tracking
• Support the development of analytical tools (e.g., dashboards and trackers) that translate raw data into actionable insights
• Gain exposure to industrial processes and materials within a climate-focused context, supporting efforts to reduce emissions in cement production (~8% of global CO₂ emissions)
• Work closely with a small, fast-paced team where your analysis and outputs will have immediate, practical impact on operations

WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

Reporting to the Program Manager, you will contribute to enhancing data integrity, visibility, and reporting to support operational performance and asset management:

Material Data Management & Dashboard Development
• Review, audit, and standardize existing material databases across multiple material categories
• Establish consistent naming conventions, units, and data structures
• Identify data gaps and support the definition of material performance criteria
• Prepare datasets for analysis and assist in the development of a Material Profile Dashboard
• Document data structures and maintain a comprehensive data dictionary

Equipment Inventory & Maintenance Management
• Conduct a comprehensive inventory of equipment and compile key asset information
• Develop and maintain a centralized equipment database
• Research and document maintenance requirements and service intervals
• Create and manage a maintenance tracking system, identifying overdue or missing activities
• Escalate data gaps, maintenance issues, and documentation deficiencies as required

Deliverables
• Standardized material databases and supporting data dictionary
• Material Profile Dashboard (draft and final)
• Equipment inventory register and maintenance tracker
• Final report summarizing activities, findings, and recommendations

WHAT YOU BRING

Required:
• Currently enrolled in a post-secondary program in Data Analytics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
• Demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data management
• Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills
• Excellent attention to detail and strong written and verbal communication skills
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a dynamic, startup environment

Nice to Have:
• Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
• Prior co-op or internship experience in an industrial, process, or manufacturing environment
• Experience building dashboards or automated reporting tools
• Interest or coursework in sustainability, low-carbon technology, or materials science
• Ability to translate data into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders

THE BIGGER PICTURE

The cement industry produces approximately 4 billion tonnes of CO₂ annually. CUT's technology has the potential to displace millions of tonnes of clinker with CO₂-sequestering SCMs — and the work you do this summer will directly advance that mission.

title: Data Engineer (12-month term position)
job_description:
Our organization

The Alberta Securities Commission (ASC) is the industry-funded regulator responsible for administering the province's securities laws. It is entrusted with fostering a fair and efficient capital market in Alberta and with protecting investors. As a member of the Canadian Securities Administrators (CSA), the ASC works to improve, coordinate and harmonize the regulation of Canada's capital markets.

The Information Technology (IT) team is responsible for making sure that the ASC has the appropriate resources necessary to ensure the consistent, reliable and secure delivery of services, and understanding and anticipating the organization's unique technology requirements.
The opportunity

Reporting to the Manager, Enterprise Information, Data & AI, this 12-month maternity leave coverage role is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the data pipelines and infrastructure that support data-driven initiatives. As the volume and complexity of our data continues to grow, this role is needed to ensure that data is accessible, reliable, and secure and play a key part in enabling data-driven decision-making across the ASC.

This role will appeal to a data engineer interested in a fixed-term role that offers practical, hands-on-work in a complex regulatory environment. It provides the opportunity to make a visible impact by enabling trusted, high-quality data to support organizational decision-making.

The ASC offers a hybrid work environment and flexibility, a competitive total rewards package consisting of 100 per cent employer-paid benefits, effective your first day of employment. These benefits include a comprehensive health and dental plan, flex days; an employee family assistance program; transportation allowance, and a generous flexible spending account. We also encourage fun and giving back to the community with initiatives offered through our ASC Social Club and annual United Way Campaign.
Key responsibilities include
• Developing efficient and scalable data pipelines to ingest/extract, transform, and load data from various sources into our data stores.
• Identifying and addressing performance bottlenecks in data pipelines and queries to ensure optimal data access.
• Monitoring data quality and implementing improvements as needed.
• Working with stakeholders to assist with data-related technical issues and supporting their data infrastructure needs.
• Developing and maintaining documentation related to data pipeline architecture, development processes and data governance standards.
• Staying current with industry trends and developments in cloud computing, data management and AI.
• Developing and updating data integration standards and best practices.
• Using DevOps Continuous Integration and Delivery (CI/CD) processes.
• Providing hands-on data integration support as part of daily operations. This includes monitoring, configuration, troubleshooting, user administration, and optimization of data objects for consumers.
• Working closely with the Data Architect to design and construct highly scalable data management platform.
• Collaborating with the Data Architect to implement effective data models and schemas.
• Working closely with cybersecurity team to establish access control in analytics solutions.
The ideal candidate will possess
• A college diploma or university degree in computer science, information systems, or computer engineering.
• A minimum of 8 years of proven experience in a Data Engineer role.
• Azure or other cloud certification (e.g. Fabric Data Engineer).
• Other data- or security-related certifications would be considered assets.
• Strong knowledge of applicable data privacy practices and regulations.
• Hands-on experience with cloud services, particularly Microsoft Fabric.
• Strong programming and query language skills in Python, SQL, KQL.
• Familiarity with federated data governance and self-serve data platforms.
• Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate non-technical requirements into technical solutions.
• Strong organizational skills including attention to detail and multitasking skills
• A results-oriented and deadline-driven approach.
• The ability to multi-task, set priorities, coordinate and schedule.
• Excellent critical thinking, analytical and decision-making capabilities.
To apply

Click the Apply For This Job Online button to submit your resume, cover letter and salary expectations by April 24, 2026. This position will work out of the ASC office located in Calgary, Alberta. You will be contacted if you are selected for an interview. More information about working at the ASC including our comprehensive Total Rewards package can be found on our website at www.asc.ca.

title: Data Engineer Lead
job_description:
Data Engineer Lead

Location: Calgary, Alberta, Canada

Model of Work: Hybrid

Are you excited by challenges? Do you enjoy working in a fast-paced, international and dynamic environment? Then now is the time to join Quorum Software, a rapidly growing company and industry leader in oil & gas transformation.

Quorum Software is the world's largest provider of digital technology focused solely on business workflows that empower the next evolution of energy. From emerging companies to supermajors, throughout every region of the globe, customers rely on Quorum's proven innovation and unmatched global expertise to streamline business operations and make data-driven decisions that optimize profitability and growth. Our industry-leading solutions are transforming energy companies across the entire value chain, helping visionary leaders evolve their organizations into modern energy companies.

Overview

We are looking for a Lead Data Engineer to join our data team and take ownership of building and scaling our enterprise data platform. In this role, you will lead a team of engineers to design, develop, and deliver a robust, production-grade data solution that serves multiple business segments and data domains.

You are someone who is equally comfortable architecting a medallion lakehouse, writing a complex PySpark transformation, mentoring a junior engineer, and collaborating with data scientists to productionize models. You bring technical depth, leadership experience, and a genuine passion for clean, reliable data.

Responsibilities

Lead a team of data engineers through the full delivery lifecycle — from architecture and design through to deployment and ongoing operations
Design and implement scalable data pipelines that ingest, transform, and serve data across Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers using Medallion Architecture
Build and maintain the organization's Databricks environment, including Unity Catalog, Delta Lake, and Databricks Workflows
Develop and maintain RESTful APIs that expose data assets to downstream consumers including analytics teams, applications, and external partners
Own pipeline reliability — establishing monitoring, alerting, data quality checks, and SLA frameworks across all ingestion patterns
Partner closely with Data Scientists to support feature engineering, model training pipelines, and the operationalization of ML models into production
Define and enforce data engineering standards, coding conventions, and peer review practices across the team
Collaborate with Data Architects and Analytics Engineers to evolve the centralized data model and ensure consistency across segments
Champion data governance practices including lineage tracking, metadata management, and access control using Azure Purview and Unity Catalog
Partner closely with our Product leaders to help guide and design needed data solutions.
And other duties as assigned.

Requirements

Leadership & Delivery

3+ years leading a data engineering team or serving as a technical lead on a major data platform initiative
Demonstrated experience delivering an end-to-end data solution — from requirements through production — across a complex, multi-source environment
Ability to manage competing priorities, communicate technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders, and keep a team aligned and productive

Databricks & Lakehouse

Deep hands-on experience with Databricks or similar tools, including Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Databricks Workflows, and cluster/job optimization
Strong understanding of Medallion Architecture (Bronze / Silver / Gold) and how to apply it pragmatically across different data domains
Proficiency in PySpark and Spark SQL for large-scale data transformation

Data Pipelines & Ingestion

Experience building and maintaining pipelines across multiple ingestion patterns — APIs, CDC, file-based, streaming (Kafka, Event Hubs), and batch
Familiarity with orchestration tools such as Apache Airflow, Databricks Workflows, or Azure Data Factory
Strong understanding of pipeline reliability patterns: idempotency, incremental loading, schema evolution, and error handling

API Development

Proven experience designing and building RESTful APIs to expose data assets, including authentication, versioning, rate limiting, and documentation
Familiarity with FastAPI, Flask, or similar Python-based API frameworks
Understanding of API gateway patterns within Azure (Azure API Management)

SQL & Azure

Expert-level SQL skills including window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and data modeling
Hands-on experience with the Azure data ecosystem: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2, Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Purview, Azure Key Vault, and Azure DevOps
Understanding of Azure networking, IAM, and security patterns as they apply to data platform architecture

Data Science Collaboration

Solid grounding in data science concepts — feature engineering, model training workflows, experiment tracking, and ML model deployment
Experience supporting or building MLflow-based experiment tracking and model registries within Databricks
Ability to translate data science requirements into reliable, scalable data infrastructure

Preferred Skills

Experience with Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft OneLake
Experience in the Oil & Gas or energy industry, particularly with operational, production, or field data
Familiarity with dbt (data build tool) for analytics engineering workflows
Exposure to real-time / streaming data patterns using Kafka, MQTT, or Azure Event Hubs
Experience with Terraform or Bicep for infrastructure-as-code on Azure
Knowledge of data mesh principles and federated data ownership models

What Success Looks Like In Year One

A production-grade Medallion Architecture is live on Databricks, serving data from multiple business segments
Pipelines are reliable, monitored, and documented — the team spends more time building than firefighting
APIs are in use by downstream consumers internally and externally with clear ownership and versioning
The team has a consistent engineering culture with defined standards, review processes, and a healthy backlog
Data Scientists have a faster path from experimentation to production

About You

You don't just write good code — you make the people around you better engineers. You care about data quality not because it's in your job description, but because bad data has real consequences. You're pragmatic about perfection and know when to ship versus when to refactor. And you're excited about building something that genuinely changes how a business understands itself.

Additional Details

Background Check: The successful candidate will need to successfully complete the following clearances: Criminal History Check, Education Verification, Employment Verification, Driver’s License Verification and passport/ID validation.
Visa Sponsorship: Employment eligibility to work with Quorum Software in Canada is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for this position. The successful candidate will be required to ensure they maintain and renew any visas or permits that grant employment eligibility where applicable.

About Quorum Software

Quorum Software connects people and information across the energy value chain. Twenty years ago, we built the first software for gas plant accountants. Pipeline operators came next, followed by land administrators, pumpers, and planners. Since 1998, Quorum has helped thousands of energy workers with business workflows that optimize profitability and growth. Our vision for the future connects the global energy ecosystem through cloud-first software, data standards, and integration. The trusted source of decision-ready data for 1,800+ companies, Quorum Software makes the essential connections that let us work better together in the connected energy workplace. For more information, visit quorumsoftware.com.

Quorum Diversity Statement: At Quorum, we are committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of belonging. We want to be the place where a diverse pool of talented people join us, stay with us and do their best work. With a diverse team of employees, we grow and learn better together. The collective sum of the individual differences, life experiences, knowledge, innovation, self-expression, and talent that our employees invest in their work represents not only part of our culture, but our reputation and our achievements. We are fully focused on equity and equality and believe deeply in diversity of race, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, ethnicity, national origin, ability, neurodiversity and all the other characteristics that make us unique.

Quorum Business Solutions and Quorum Software are Equal Opportunity Employers. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, ancestry, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or any other basis protected by law.

Those applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application and/or interview process should notify a member of the Human Resources Department

All legitimate recruitment activities are conducted exclusively through our official careers website (www.quorumsoftware.com/careers) and email addresses ending in @quorumsoftware.com. title: Senior Data Engineer- Fabric Platform

Krux builds innovative SAAS solutions for the mining industry. We empower our customers to make better decisions through real-time data management and analytics. title: Data Engineering Specialist
job_description:

As a Canadian leader in infrastructure development, Aecon is safely and sustainably building what matters for future generations to thrive! We lead some of the most impactful infrastructure projects of our generation, at the forefront of transformational change in transportation and energy, and partnering every day to build, connect, power, and strengthen our communities.

As the Data Engineering Specialist, you will be versatile and driven to help build the data foundation for our AI/ML initiatives. This role is essential to enabling intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and machine learning across our operations. You'll design scalable data pipelines, transform complex data sources, and ensure our models have the high-quality inputs they need to deliver real impact.

What You'll Do Here:

Scalable Data Ingestion
• Develop and maintain robust pipelines for ingesting data from diverse sources, including IoT devices, operational systems, and field applications.
• Ensure data is clean, timely, and reliable for downstream analytics and modeling.

System Integration
• Build connectors and workflows to integrate with various enterprise platforms and tools.

Structured/Unstructured Data Processing

AI/ML Enablement

• Build feature pipelines, manage dataset versioning, and track data lineage.

Operational Resilience
• Strengthen system reliability and reduce technical debt by supporting ingestion, integration, and data infrastructure.
• Monitor and optimize performance across data workflows.

Strategic Impact

• Ensure the AI/ML team has the data infrastructure needed to deliver high-impact solutions.

• 3+ years of experience using Databricks and/or MS Fabric
• Proficiency in Python, SQL, and distributed data frameworks (e.g., Spark, Kafka).
• Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP) and orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Prefect).
• Strong understanding of data modeling, ETL/ELT processes, and unstructured data workflows.

Pay Range: $81,500 - $95,000 per year

title: Software Engineer, Data Infrastructure
job_description:
The mission of Speechify is to make sure that reading is never a barrier to learning.

Over 50 million people use Speechify’s text-to-speech products to turn whatever they’re reading – PDFs, books, Google Docs, news articles, websites – into audio, so they can read faster, read more, and remember more. Speechify’s text-to-speech reading products include its iOS app, Android App, Mac App, Chrome Extension, and Web App. Google recently named Speechify the Chrome Extension of the Year and Apple named Speechify its 2025 Design Award winner for Inclusivity.

Today, nearly 200 people around the globe work on Speechify in a 100% distributed setting – Speechify has no office. These include frontend and backend engineers, AI research scientists, and others from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, leading PhD programs like Stanford, high growth startups like Stripe, Vercel, Bolt, and many founders of their own companies.

Overview

We're looking to hire for our Data side of our AI team at Speechify. This role is responsible for all aspects of data collection to support our model training operations. We are able to build high-quality datasets at petabyte-scale and low cost through a tight integration of infrastructure, engineering, and research work. We are looking for a skilled Software Engineer to join us.

What You’ll Do
• Be scrappy to find new sources of audio data and bring it into our ingestion pipeline
• Operate and extend the cloud infrastructure for our ingestion pipeline, currently running on GCP and managed with Terraform.
• Collaborate closely with our Scientists to shift the cost/throughput/quality frontier, delivering richer data at bigger scale and lower cost to power our next-generation models.
• Collaborate with others on the AI Team and Speechify Leadership to craft the AI Team’s dataset roadmap to power Speechify’s next-generation consumer and enterprise products.

An Ideal Candidate Should Have
• BS/MS/PhD in Computer Science or a related field.
• 5+ years of industry experience in software development.
• Proficiency with bash/Python scripting in Linux environments
• Proficiency in Docker and Infrastructure-as-Code concepts and professional experience with at least one major Cloud Provider (we use GCP)
• Experience with web crawlers, large-scale data processing workflows is a plus
• Ability to handle multiple tasks and adapt to changing priorities.
• Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

What we offer
• A fast-growing environment where you can help shape the company and product.
• An entrepreneurial-minded team that supports risk, intuition, and hustle.
• A hands-off management approach so you can focus and do your best work.
• An opportunity to make a big impact in a transformative industry.
• Competitive salaries, a friendly and laid-back atmosphere, and a commitment to building a great asynchronous culture.
• Opportunity to work on a life-changing product that millions of people use.
• Build products that directly impact and support people with learning differences like dyslexia, ADD, low vision, concussions, autism, and more.
• Work in one of the fastest-growing sectors of tech, the intersection of artificial intelligence and audio.

And don’t forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.

Refer them

title: Business Intelligence Developer
job_description:
Job Description:

McCarthy Tétrault is a Canadian law firm that offers a full suite of legal and business solutions to clients in Canada and around the world. At McCarthy Tétrault, we offer challenging and rewarding career opportunities in our offices in Toronto, Montreal, Quebec City, Calgary and Vancouver. We are delighted to have earned the distinction of one of Canada’s Best Diversity Employers for the fourteenth consecutive year in 2026. Our culture is built on professional excellence, collaboration, innovation, thought leadership and entrepreneurialism. We embrace inclusion in all its forms and we provide the tools and opportunities to help our people develop to their full potential.

We are recruiting for a Business Intelligence Developer to join our team in our Vancouver, Montreal, Calgary or Toronto office. The successful candidate must have a minimum of 3 years of experience in data visualization, data modeling and process automation, and will be responsible for creating interactive dashboards, developing and automating finance processes and reports, and managing data across platforms. McCarthy Tétrault employees benefit from a hybrid work environment.

As a Business Intelligence Developer, you will be:
• Developing interactive dashboards, alerts and reporting templates to offer real-time actionable insights to stakeholders within the Finance department and to other internal stakeholders (Senior Leadership, Partners, etc.).
• Leading the ongoing development and automation of finance reports, digital forms and processes, enhancing self-serve capabilities for internal stakeholders through BI tools and platforms, optimizing the end-user experience.
• Managing, updating, and validating data across multiple platforms, ensuring accuracy and consistency.
• Executing both routine and ad hoc reporting encompassing various firm dimensions like practice, region, industry, timekeeper, and more.
• Providing technical expertise for the firm's finance digitization initiative, playing a pivotal role in its successful realization.
• Planning and executing project management processes like requirements gathering, process mapping, quality assurance, rollout strategies, communication strategies, and change management plans for the firm’s finance digitization efforts.
• Producing practical demonstrations showcasing the utilization of products tailored to our unique environments.
• Building and maintaining strong relationships with internal (billing analysts, billing lawyers, paralegals, legal assistants, etc.) and external stakeholders, offering them comprehensive support and insights.

As our ideal candidate, you will distinguish yourself by the following profile:
• University degree in a related field (e.g. Data Science, Computer Science, Finance or Engineering).
• Minimum 3 years of relevant work experience, preferably acquired within a legal or professional services environment.
• Experience building data models and dashboards with Power BI (preferred, including complex DAX queries and paginated reports), SSRS (preferred) or similar BI tools (Tableau, Qlik, etc.).
• Proficiency with TSQL, with an emphasis on intricate query formulation. Familiarity with database maintenance is valued.
• Capability to design automated alert systems using tools like SSRS and Power Automate.
• Knowledge of enterprise solutions such as Power Apps and ServiceNow, and coding languages like Python & JavaScript.
• Mastery in MS Excel, inclusive of VBA scripting for report automation.
• Familiarity with AI agents creation (e.g. using Copilot Studio) for process automation would be an asset.
• Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills; adept at creating meaningful insights from source data.
• Very strong written and communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to summarize and convey complex information in a clear and concise manner. Capacity to demonstrate tact and diplomacy when dealing with internal and external clients and respect confidentiality.
• Strong ability to prioritize concurrent activities, follow through on open items and provide status updates, unprompted. Deadline oriented.
• Self-Starter & quick learner – willing to spend time investigating data on your own to minimize disruption to SMEs.

As a member of the McCarthy team, you will have access to:
• Outstanding benefits from day one, including insurance premiums paid by the Firm and wellness and technology reimbursements.
• Competitive compensation, paid overtime and generous time off, including a day off to volunteer and a day off for your birthday.
• A commitment to professional development and growth opportunities for our people at all levels, supported by a culture that fully embraces and encourages two-way feedback.
• Strong community involvement and a commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.
• A collaborative, cohesive culture that connects lawyers and business teams through collective purpose.

How to Apply:

We encourage external candidates to apply online and internal applicants must apply directly through our internal careers portal on Espresso. We look forward to receiving your application.

We thank all applicants for their interest in McCarthy Tétrault; however, only chosen applicants will be contacted. We regret that we are unable to respond to individual inquiries about application status. McCarthy Tétrault is an equal opportunity employer that fosters an inclusive, equitable, and accessible environment. Please notify us if you require accommodation at any time during the recruitment process.

title: BI Engineer
job_description:
About NDT Global

NDT Global is the leading provider of inline diagnostic solutions, advanced data insights, subsea inspection, and integrity management services that safeguard energy-sector infrastructure. The company is recognized for its expertise in both ultrasonic inspection technologies—such as Pulse Echo, Pitch-and-Catch, Phased Array, and Acoustic Resonance (ART Scan)—and ultra-high-resolution Magnetic Flux Leakage (MFL) inspection services. These differentiated offerings, along with non-ultrasonic technologies like Inertial Measurement Units and others in development, enable NDT Global to deliver comprehensive asset integrity solutions.

Innovation is at the core of NDT Global's mission. We continuously challenge the boundaries of existing technologies to deliver transformational solutions that empower the industry to achieve safer, more cost-effective pipeline management. Our commitment to research and development drives the creation of vital new methods and tools that address the evolving needs of our customers while setting new industry standards.

By strategically applying inspection technologies to detect, diagnose, and model various types of threats, NDT Global provides predictive, decision-ready insights. These insights, driven by the world’s most accurate data, enable asset owners to optimize infrastructure health, drive operational efficiencies, reduce risk, and minimize their carbon footprint

The BI Engineer is responsible for the development and maintenance of database systems and the ongoing analysis of NDT-Global and industry specific data, obtained from a variety of sources.
The role requires proficiency with analytical tools, involves the ability to analyze complex data from conceptualization through presentation to the internal stakeholders and external client
Responsibilities
• Develop Reports both graphical and table based in an Analytics & BI Platform environment such as Power BI
• Database Design &Development in a Microsoft SQL Server environment
• Develop ETL solutions in a MS Visual Studio environment or equivalent
• Business and data requirements gathering and translating these into software solutions
• Responsible for testing, ensuring good coding standards
• Manage expectations from stakeholders, able to set and adjust priorities as needed
• Build predictive analytics by extracting and aggregating data
• Writing of technical documents

Qualifications and requirements
• A bachelor's degree in computer science or related discipline is required
• 4 years or more professional experience in developing database systems and analysis of heterogeneous data sources is required
• Strong analytical background is essential. Experience in predictive analytics and statistics is an advantage.
• Ability to demonstrate working with ETL solutions is preferred
• Advanced understanding of Microsoft SQL Server & T-SQL
• Intermediate understanding of Microsoft Integration Services (SSIS)
• Intermediate understanding of Power BI / Microsoft Reporting Services (SSRS)
• Basic understanding of Microsoft Access or Oracle databases
• Basic understanding of TFS or Git source control

Skillset
• Ability to write clear and structured technical documentation

• Skilled at setting and adjusting priorities in a dynamic environment
